# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com

### Essential Commands (Run on VM)

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npm run lint                    # ESLint

# Database
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # After deploy
```

---

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
├── config/
│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
```

---

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

---

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
           └── 1:N Round
                     └── N:1 Course (optional)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

---

## Adding a New Endpoint

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests and run
npm run test
```

---

## Updating Prisma Schema

```bash
# 1. Edit prisma/schema.prisma
# 2. Format
npx prisma format

# 3. Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_thing_field

# 4. Regenerate client
npx prisma generate

# 5. Update DTOs and services

# 6. Test
npm run test
```

---

## Environment Variables

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
DIRECT_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

---

## CORS Configuration

Location: `src/main.ts`

```typescript
origin: [
  'https://lazygolfing.com',
  'https://www.lazygolfing.com',
  'https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev',
  // Add for local dev: 'http://localhost:8080',
]
```

---

## Validation Rules (DTOs)

### Profile (update-profile.dto.ts)
- `firstName`: 1-50 chars
- `lastName`: 1-50 chars
- `ghinNumber`: 7 digits (string)
- `handicap`: 0-54 (float)

### Round (create-round.dto.ts)
- `courseName`: 1-100 chars (required)
- `strokes`: positive integer (required)
- `date`: ISO 8601 datetime (optional)
- `notes`: max 500 chars (optional)

---

## Debugging

```bash
# Check logs
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100

# Test endpoints
curl http://192.168.1.40:3000/
curl http://192.168.1.40:3000/auth/session
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=..." http://192.168.1.40:3000/v1/me

# Production
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

### Common Issues

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| Migration failed | DB not running | Check PostgreSQL status |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Check cookie, re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to main.ts allowlist |
| 500 Internal Server Error | Uncaught exception | Check PM2 logs |

---

## Deployment

```bash
# 1. SSH to VM
# 2. Pull latest code
git pull origin main

# 3. Install dependencies
npm ci

# 4. Run migrations
npx prisma migrate deploy

# 5. Rebuild
npm run build

# 6. Restart PM2
pm2 restart lg-api

# 7. Verify
curl http://localhost:3000/
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 20
```

### PM2 Config (ecosystem.config.js)

```javascript
{
  name: 'lg-api',
  script: 'dist/src/main.js',
  instances: 1,
  exec_mode: 'fork',
  autorestart: true,
  max_restarts: 25,
  max_memory_restart: '300M',
}
```

---

## Security Checklist

Before deploying new endpoints:
- [ ] Uses `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` if protected
- [ ] Input validated with class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data
- [ ] Sensitive data filtered from responses
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs

---

*For root-level documentation, see [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md)*
*For frontend documentation, see [/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.home/CLAUDE.md)*

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com

### Essential Commands (Run on VM)

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npm run lint                    # ESLint

# Database
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # After deploy
```

---

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
├── config/
│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
```

---

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

---

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
           └── 1:N Round
                     └── N:1 Course (optional)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

---

## Adding a New Endpoint

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests and run
npm run test
```

---

## Updating Prisma Schema

```bash
# 1. Edit prisma/schema.prisma
# 2. Format
npx prisma format

# 3. Create migration
npx prisma migrate dev --name add_thing_field

# 4. Regenerate client
npx prisma generate

# 5. Update DTOs and services

# 6. Test
npm run test
```

---

## Environment Variables

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
DIRECT_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>.apps.googleusercontent.com
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=GOCSPX-<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

---

## CORS Configuration

Location: `src/main.ts`

```typescript
origin: [
  'https://lazygolfing.com',
  'https://www.lazygolfing.com',
  'https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev',
  // Add for local dev: 'http://localhost:8080',
]
```

---

## Validation Rules (DTOs)

### Profile (update-profile.dto.ts)

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `firstName` | string | Optional, 1-50 chars |
| `lastName` | string | Optional, 1-50 chars |
| `ghinNumber` | string | Optional, empty → null |
| `handicap` | number/null | Optional, -10 to 54 |

### Round (create-round.dto.ts)

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `courseId` | string | Optional, UUID FK to Course |
| `courseName` | string | Optional, max 100 chars |
| `strokes` | number | **Required**, 18-200 |
| `front9` | number | Optional, 9-100 |
| `back9` | number | Optional, 9-100 |
| `playingPartners` | string | Optional, CSV names |
| `date` | string | Optional, ISO date |
| `teeTime` | string | Optional, ISO datetime |
| `latitude` | number | Optional, -90 to 90 |
| `longitude` | number | Optional, -180 to 180 |
| `notes` | string | Optional, max 500 chars |

### Advanced Round Stats (all optional)

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `girPct` | number | 0-100 (Greens in Regulation %) |
| `firPct` | number | 0-100 (Fairways in Regulation %) |
| `approachPct` | number | 0-100 |
| `front9Putts` | number | 0-50 |
| `back9Putts` | number | 0-50 |
| `totalPutts` | number | 0-100 |
| `holeInOnes` | number | 0+ |
| `eagles` | number | 0+ |
| `birdies` | number | 0+ |
| `pars` | number | 0+ |
| `bogeys` | number | 0+ |
| `doubleBogeys` | number | 0+ |
| `triplePlusBogeys` | number | 0+ |

### Course (create-course.dto.ts)

| Field | Type | Constraints |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `name` | string | **Required** |
| `city` | string | Optional |
| `state` | string | Optional |
| `country` | string | Optional |
| `totalPar` | number | Optional |
| `courseRating` | number | Optional |
| `slopeRating` | number | Optional |

---

## Debugging

```bash
# Check logs
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100

# Test endpoints
curl http://192.168.1.40:3000/
curl http://192.168.1.40:3000/auth/session
curl -H "Cookie: lg_jwt=..." http://192.168.1.40:3000/v1/me

### Common Issues

## Deployment

### PM2 Config (ecosystem.config.js)

```javascript
{
  name: 'lg-api',

---

## Security Checklist

Before deploying new endpoints:
- [ ] Uses `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` if protected

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home)

> AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration)

---

## Quick Reference

### Tech Stack
- **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19
- **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4
- **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style)
- **State:** Zustand (when needed)
- **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark)

### Environment
- **Code editing:** Windows machine
- **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000`
- **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages)

### Essential Commands

```bash
# Development
npm install             # Install dependencies
npm run dev             # Start dev server (localhost:3000)
npm run build           # Build for production (static export)
npm run start           # Serve production build locally
npm run lint            # ESLint checks

# Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site)
npm run build:data      # CSV → JSON conversion
npm run check:data      # Verify JSON is current
```

---

## Project Structure

```
lzg.home/
├── src/
│   ├── app/                    # Next.js App Router
│   │   ├── layout.tsx          # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema)
│   │   ├── page.tsx            # Home page (content only)
│   │   ├── globals.css         # Global styles + CSS variables
│   │   ├── about/page.tsx      # About page (uses main layout)
│   │   ├── (auth)/             # Auth route group (minimal layout)
│   │   │   ├── layout.tsx      # Auth-specific layout (no full nav)
│   │   │   └── login/page.tsx  # Login page
│   │   └── [feature]/          # Feature routes
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── layout/             # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE)
│   │   │   ├── Header.tsx      # Site header with nav
│   │   │   ├── Footer.tsx      # Site footer
│   │   │   └── index.ts        # Barrel export
│   │   ├── seo/                # SEO components
│   │   │   ├── JsonLd.tsx      # Schema.org structured data
│   │   │   └── index.ts        # Barrel export
│   │   ├── ui/                 # shadcn/ui components
│   │   ├── theme-provider.tsx  # next-themes wrapper
│   │   └── theme-toggle.tsx    # Dark/light toggle
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── utils.ts            # cn() utility for class merging
│   │   ├── metadata.ts         # Centralized SEO metadata config
│   │   ├── theme.ts            # Brand colors configuration
│   │   └── auth.ts             # Authentication utilities
│   └── hooks/                  # Custom React hooks
├── public/
│   ├── alpha/                  # Legacy static site (v1)
│   ├── favicons/               # All favicon files (organized)
│   ├── images/                 # Logo, OG image, photos
│   ├── patterns/               # SVG patterns (grid, etc.)
│   └── site.webmanifest        # PWA manifest
├── next.config.ts              # Next.js config (static export)
├── tailwind.config.ts          # Tailwind + theme colors
├── tsconfig.json               # TypeScript config
├── components.json             # shadcn/ui config
└── package.json                # Dependencies & scripts
```

---

## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns)

### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer

**RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages.

```tsx
// src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern
import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout';

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return (

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

---

## API Integration

### Backend Communication
The new site will use the same API as the legacy site:

```typescript
// src/lib/api.ts (to be created)
const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com';

export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) {
  const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, {
    credentials: 'include',  // For JWT cookie
    ...options,
  });
  return response.json();
}

// Usage:
const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session');
const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me');
```

### Environment Variables
Create `.env.local` for development:
```
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000
```

For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`.

---

## Routing

### App Router Structure
```
src/app/
├── layout.tsx            # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema)
├── page.tsx              # / (home) - content only
├── about/page.tsx        # /about
├── (auth)/               # Auth route group (minimal header)
│   ├── layout.tsx        # Auth-specific layout
│   └── login/page.tsx    # /login
├── (main)/               # Future: Pages with full nav
│   ├── rounds/
│   │   ├── page.tsx      # /rounds (list)
│   │   └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail)
│   ├── leagues/

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ ├── api.ts # API client (auto-detects env) │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks (future) ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### Backend Communication The new site will use the same API as the legacy site: ```typescript // src/lib/api.ts (to be created) const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com'; export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) { const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, { credentials: 'include', // For JWT cookie ...options, }); return response.json(); } // Usage: const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session'); const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me'); ``` ### Environment Variables Create `.env.local` for development: ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`. --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. // Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ ├── api.ts # API client (auto-detects env) │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks (future) ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### API Client (`src/lib/api.ts`) ```typescript import { apiRequest, API_BASE, IS_DEV } from '@/lib/api'; // GET request const { ok, data } = await apiRequest('/v1/endpoint'); // POST with body const result = await apiRequest('/v1/rounds', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ courseName: 'Test', strokes: 85 }), }); // Response type interface ApiResponse { ok: boolean; status: number; data: T | null; } ``` **Features:** - Auto-detects localhost vs production - Includes credentials (for JWT cookies) - CSRF token extraction from cookies - Handles 204 No Content responses ### Authentication (`src/lib/auth.ts`) ```typescript import { getSession, getMe, logout, getGoogleLoginUrl } from '@/lib/auth'; // Check auth status const session = await getSession(); if (session.authenticated) { console.log(session.user.email); } // Get user profile const { ok, data: profile } = await getMe(); // Redirect to Google OAuth window.location.href = getGoogleLoginUrl(); ``` ### Environment Variables | Variable | Purpose | Default | |----------|---------|---------| | `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend API URL | Auto-detects based on hostname | **Auto-detection logic:** - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use that - If hostname is `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` → use `http://localhost:3000` - Otherwise → use `https://api.lazygolfing.com` **Development `.env.local`:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation - **Root:** [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) - **Backend:** [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - **Design System:** [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) - **Feature Roadmap:** [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - **Architecture:** [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

content.content:
1→# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform
     2→
     3→> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo
     4→
     5→**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Quick Reference
    10→
    11→### Most-Used Commands
    12→
    13→```bash
    14→# Frontend (lzg.home/)
    15→npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
    16→npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
    17→npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
    18→npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
    19→npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current
    20→
