file.content:
# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | Check login status |
| `GET /v1/me` | Get user profile (auth required) |
| `PUT /v1/me` | Update profile (auth required) |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | List rounds (auth required) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | Create round (auth required) |

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |
| `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` | Next.js/React patterns | Working on frontend |
| `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` | API/database details | Working on backend |

---

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

---

## Critical Rules

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
5. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens

### Before Completing Tasks

- [ ] Backend: Tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] Frontend: Data check passes (`npm run check:data`)
- [ ] No security issues introduced
- [ ] Code follows existing patterns

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

---

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

---

## Environment Variables

### Backend (.env)

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

```
https://lazygolfing.com
https://www.lazygolfing.com
https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
```

For local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

---

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

---

## Documentation Links

### Development Resources

- [Master Todo List](./docs/todo/MASTER_TODO.md)
- [Technical Debt Register](./docs/technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md)
- [System Architecture](./docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md)

### Planning Resources

- [Product Roadmap](./docs/plans/PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md)

### Project-Specific Guides

- [Frontend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) - Next.js 15, React 19, Miami Vice theme
- [Feature Roadmap](./docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - Comprehensive feature list
- [Backend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - API endpoints, Prisma, authentication

---

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

---

## Brand Guidelines

### Voice
- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
- Celebrate small wins and funny losses

### Miami Vice Color Palette

| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

See [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.

---

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
├── favicons/     # All favicon files
├── images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
├── patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
└── fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

### Social Sharing Standards

| Standard | Specification |
|----------|--------------|
| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

### Documentation Hierarchy

```
/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules
/docs/                        # Detailed documentation
  ├── plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists
  ├── research/               # Market research, competitors
  ├── technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking
  └── architecture/           # System design
/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules
/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md       # Backend-specific rules
```

### Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| React Components | PascalCase | `Header.tsx`, `CourseCard.tsx` |
| Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `formatDate.ts` |
| API Routes | kebab-case | `/v1/golf-rounds` |
| CSS Files | kebab-case | `button-styles.css` |
| Assets | kebab-case | `og-image.jpg`, `logo-dark.png` |

---

*For detailed project-specific guidance, see the CLAUDE.md files in each subdirectory.*

# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | Check login status |
| `GET /v1/me` | Get user profile (auth required) |
| `PUT /v1/me` | Update profile (auth required) |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | List rounds (auth required) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | Create round (auth required) |

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |
| `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` | Next.js/React patterns | Working on frontend |
| `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` | API/database details | Working on backend |

---

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

---

## Critical Rules

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
5. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens

### Before Completing Tasks

- [ ] Backend: Tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] Frontend: Data check passes (`npm run check:data`)
- [ ] No security issues introduced
- [ ] Code follows existing patterns

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

---

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

---

## Environment Variables

### Backend (.env)

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

```
https://lazygolfing.com
https://www.lazygolfing.com
https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
```

For local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

---

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

---

## Documentation Links

### Development Resources

- [Master Todo List](./docs/todo/MASTER_TODO.md)
- [Technical Debt Register](./docs/technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md)
- [System Architecture](./docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md)

### Planning Resources

- [Product Roadmap](./docs/plans/PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md)

### Project-Specific Guides

- [Frontend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) - Next.js 15, React 19, Miami Vice theme
- [Feature Roadmap](./docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) - Comprehensive feature list
- [Backend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) - API endpoints, Prisma, authentication

---

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

---

## Brand Guidelines

### Voice
- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
- Celebrate small wins and funny losses

### Miami Vice Color Palette

| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

See [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.

---

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
├── favicons/     # All favicon files
├── images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
├── patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
└── fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

### Social Sharing Standards

| Standard | Specification |
|----------|--------------|
| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

### Documentation Hierarchy

```
/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules
/docs/                        # Detailed documentation
  ├── plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists
  ├── research/               # Market research, competitors
  ├── technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking
  └── architecture/           # System design
/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules
/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md       # Backend-specific rules
```

### Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| React Components | PascalCase | `Header.tsx`, `CourseCard.tsx` |
| Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `formatDate.ts` |
| API Routes | kebab-case | `/v1/golf-rounds` |
| CSS Files | kebab-case | `button-styles.css` |
| Assets | kebab-case | `og-image.jpg`, `logo-dark.png` |

---

*For detailed project-specific guidance, see the CLAUDE.md files in each subdirectory.*

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | None | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| `POST /auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
| `GET /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Get user profile |
| `PUT /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Update profile |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | List rounds (paginated) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | Create round |
| `GET /v1/courses/search` | None | Search courses |

See [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) for complete endpoint documentation.

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |

```bash

cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment

npx prisma migrate dev

## Critical Rules

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)`**
4. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
5. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens

```bash

# 2. Regenerate JSON

1. **First User Request (Favicon Setup):**

   - User requested the favicons be set up properly with a single source of truth

   - Copied favicon files to `lzg.home/public/favicons/`
   - Updated `site.webmanifest` with correct paths
   - Updated `metadata.ts` to include new favicon configuration (96x96, SVG, etc.)

   - Verified build works, committed and pushed

3. **Second User Question (OG Image):**
   - User asked if OG image was also set up with single source of truth
   - I confirmed it was already centralized in `metadata.ts`

4. **Third User Request (Documentation Review - CRITICAL FEEDBACK):**

We need to do a full comprehensive review of the entire workspace (root, lzg.home & lzg.backend) and make sure ALL documentation is fully updated and as detailed as can be. Please expand current information and create new documentation where necessary. Only delete/edit sections that you know for 100% fact need to be changed."
   - This was important user feedback - they wanted MORE comprehensive documentation, not just favicon-related updates

5. **My Actions for Comprehensive Documentation:**
   - Used Task subagents to explore lzg.home, lzg.backend, and docs folders comprehensively
   - Fixed docs/README.md to match actual file structure

1. Primary Request and Intent:

   - **Follow-up:** Confirm OG image has same single source of truth setup
   - **Critical Request (User Feedback):** Comprehensive documentation review of entire workspace (root, lzg.home, lzg.backend). User explicitly criticized my narrow approach: "Why did you only update the CLAUDE.md files and only regarding the favicon and seo stuff?" User wanted ALL documentation fully updated, expanded, with new documentation created where necessary, and only deleting sections known to need changes.

content.text:
I see - the repos are separate Git repositories. The docs folder is at the root level which doesn't have its own Git repo. The workspace is organized as:
- `lzg.home/` - separate git repo (frontend)
- `lzg.backend/` - separate git repo (backend)
- `docs/` - not tracked in a git repo (shared documentation)

Let me commit and push the changes in each repo. I'll start with lzg.home:

input.command: cd "D:\Proton Drive\My files\Documents\Professional\Programming\Web Development\.lzg_platform\Lazy Golfing\lzg.home" && git diff CLAUDE.md

@@ -370,34 +371,68 @@ import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
 
 ## API Integration
 
-### Backend Communication
-The new site will use the same API as the legacy site:
+### API Client (`src/lib/api.ts`)
 
 ```typescript
-// src/lib/api.ts (to be created)
-const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.lazygolfing.com';
-
-export async function apiRequest(path: string, options?: RequestInit) {
-  const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}${path}`, {
-    credentials: 'include',  // For JWT cookie
-    ...options,
-  });
-  return response.json();
+import { apiRequest, API_BASE, IS_DEV } from '@/lib/api';
+
+// GET request
+const { ok, data } = await apiRequest<MyType>('/v1/endpoint');
+
+// POST with body
+const result = await apiRequest('/v1/rounds', {
+  method: 'POST',
+  body: JSON.stringify({ courseName: 'Test', strokes: 85 }),
+});
+
+// Response type
+interface ApiResponse<T> {
+  ok: boolean;
+  status: number;
+  data: T | null;
+}
+```
+
+**Features:**
+- Auto-detects localhost vs production
+- Includes credentials (for JWT cookies)
+- CSRF token extraction from cookies
+- Handles 204 No Content responses
+
+### Authentication (`src/lib/auth.ts`)
+
+```typescript
+import { getSession, getMe, logout, getGoogleLoginUrl } from '@/lib/auth';
+
+// Check auth status
+const session = await getSession();
+if (session.authenticated) {
+  console.log(session.user.email);
 }
 
-// Usage:
-const session = await apiRequest('/auth/session');
-const profile = await apiRequest('/v1/me');
+// Get user profile
+const { ok, data: profile } = await getMe();
+
+// Redirect to Google OAuth
+window.location.href = getGoogleLoginUrl();
 ```
 
 ### Environment Variables
-Create `.env.local` for development:
+
+| Variable | Purpose | Default |
+|----------|---------|---------|
+| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend API URL | Auto-detects based on hostname |
+
+**Auto-detection logic:**
+- If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use that
+- If hostname is `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` → use `http://localhost:3000`
+- Otherwise → use `https://api.lazygolfing.com`
+
+**Development `.env.local`:**
 ```
 NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000
 ```
 
-For production, the default falls back to `https://api.lazygolfing.com`.
-
 ---
 
 ## Routing

+### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)
 
 | Method | Path | Description |

@@ -152,15 +175,61 @@ AuditLog (standalone)
 ### Protecting Routes
 
 ```typescript
+// Basic JWT protection
 @Get('protected-route')
-@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
+@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
 async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
-  // user contains JWT payload
+  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
+}
+
+// Admin-only route
+@Put('admin-route')
+@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
+async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
+  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
 }
 ```
 
 ---
 
+## CSRF Protection
+
+### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern
+
+Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.
+
+**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
+**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
+**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)
+
+```typescript
+// Frontend request example
+fetch('/v1/me', {
+  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
+  headers: {
+    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
+  }
+});
+```
+
+**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests
+
+---
+
+## Rate Limiting
+
+Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:
+
+| Category | Limit | Window |
+|----------|-------|--------|
+| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
+| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
+| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |
+
+**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`
+
+---
+
 ## Adding a New Endpoint

@@ -249,16 +318,59 @@ origin: [
 ## Validation Rules (DTOs)
 
 ### Profile (update-profile.dto.ts)
-- `firstName`: 1-50 chars
-- `lastName`: 1-50 chars
-- `ghinNumber`: 7 digits (string)
-- `handicap`: 0-54 (float)
+
+| Field | Type | Constraints |
+|-------|------|-------------|
+| `firstName` | string | Optional, 1-50 chars |
+| `lastName` | string | Optional, 1-50 chars |
+| `ghinNumber` | string | Optional, empty → null |
+| `handicap` | number/null | Optional, -10 to 54 |
 
 ### Round (create-round.dto.ts)
-- `courseName`: 1-100 chars (required)

 ### Protecting Routes
 
 ```typescript
+// Basic JWT protection
 @Get('protected-route')
-@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
+@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
 async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
-  // user contains JWT payload
+  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
+}
+
+// Admin-only route
+@Put('admin-route')
+@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
+async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
+  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
 }
 ```
 
 ---
 
+## CSRF Protection
+
+### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern
+
+Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.
+
+**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
+**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
+**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)
+
+```typescript
+// Frontend request example
+fetch('/v1/me', {
+  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
+  headers: {
+    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
+  }
+});
+```
+
+**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests
+
+---
+
+## Rate Limiting
+
+Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:
+
+| Category | Limit | Window |
+|----------|-------|--------|
+| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
+| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
+| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |
+
+**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`
+

content.text:
# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes your task and automatically routes to the optimal execution strategy per CLAUDE.md's Intelligent Prompt Routing system.

## Routing Logic

**If no task description provided:**
- Run `/check-todos` to list outstanding work
- Await user selection

**If task description provided, analyze and route:**

### 1. Planning Tasks → `/create-plan`
**Keywords:** "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase", "roadmap"
**Complexity:** Multi-phase features, complex architecture
**Example:** "Plan the analytics dashboard feature"

→ Invoke: `/create-plan {{task_description}}`

### 2. Decision-Making → `/consider:*` frameworks
**Keywords:** "should we", "which approach", "evaluate", "compare", "decide"
**Examples:**
- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`

→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework

### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"

→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description

### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
**Criteria:**
- 30min - 2hr estimated work
- 3-10 files to modify
- Clear scope but needs fresh context
**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"

→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`

### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"

→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`

### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
**Criteria:**
- 1-2 files
- <30min work
- Clear, straightforward
**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"

→ Execute directly in current context

## Implementation

**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
```javascript
const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();

if (!taskDescription) {
  // No task provided - check todos
  return "/check-todos";
}
```

**Step 2: Analyze Task**

Examine task description for:
- **Keywords** indicating task type
- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)

**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**

Based on analysis:

```javascript
// Planning tasks
if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
}

// Decision-making tasks
else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
}

// Debugging tasks
else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
}

// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
  // Offer to run after creation
}

// Multi-stage features
else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
}

// Simple/direct - execute in current context
else {
  executeDirectly();
}
```

**Step 4: Follow Through**

After routing:
- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
- If framework: present analysis and next steps

## Examples

```bash
# Automatically routes to /create-plan
/start-task Plan the user authentication system

# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?

# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure

# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
/start-task Refactor the calendar view components

# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach

# Direct execution
/start-task Fix typo in README.md

# Check todos (no argument)
/start-task
```

## Notes

- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed

Rules & Critical Priorities: Foundational, Stable, Lean, Clean, UX/UI, Security, Retention, Efficient, Optimized, Updated, Knowledgeable, Familiar w/ Industry, and anything else you can think of that will help us develop the best platform possible.

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md - Lazy Golfing Platform

> AI assistant guidelines for the LazyGolfing monorepo

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## Quick Reference

### Most-Used Commands

```bash
# Frontend (lzg.home/)
npm run dev                     # Development server (localhost:3000)
npm run build                   # Production build (static export)
npm run lint                    # ESLint checks
npm run build:data              # CSV → JSON (legacy /alpha site)
npm run check:data              # Verify JSON is current

# Backend (lzg.backend/) - Run on VM (192.168.1.40)
npm run start:dev               # Development server with hot-reload
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema.prisma changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # Database GUI (192.168.1.40:5555)

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View backend logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # Restart after deploy
```

### Key API Endpoints

| Endpoint | Auth | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `GET /auth/google` | None | Start OAuth flow |
| `GET /auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| `POST /auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
| `GET /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Get user profile |
| `PUT /v1/me` | JWT+CSRF | Update profile |
| `GET /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | List rounds (paginated) |
| `POST /v1/rounds` | JWT+CSRF | Create round |
| `GET /v1/courses/search` | None | Search courses |

See [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) for complete endpoint documentation.

### Test Commands

```bash
# Test API connectivity
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

---

## Repository Structure

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                    # This file - root AI instructions
├── docs/                        # Centralized documentation
│   ├── plans/                   # Product roadmap, feature roadmap
│   ├── todo/                    # Task tracking
│   ├── technical-debt/          # Code quality issues
│   ├── design/                  # Design system documentation
│   └── architecture/            # System design docs
├── media/                       # Brand assets (not deployed)
│   ├── logos/                   # Official logos and variants
│   ├── kikman/                  # Kikman sub-brand assets
│   └── archive/                 # Historical planning screenshots
├── lzg.home/                    # Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19)
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Frontend-specific AI instructions
│   ├── src/app/                # Next.js App Router pages
│   ├── src/components/         # React components + shadcn/ui
│   ├── src/lib/theme.ts        # Centralized color config
│   └── public/alpha/           # Legacy static site (preserved)
└── lzg.backend/                 # Backend (NestJS API)
    ├── CLAUDE.md               # Backend-specific AI instructions
    └── README.md               # Backend documentation
```

### Documentation Hierarchy

| File | Purpose | When to Read |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `/CLAUDE.md` | Quick reference, critical rules | Always |
| `/docs/` | Detailed documentation | Planning, research |
| `/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md` | Next.js/React patterns | Working on frontend |
| `/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md` | API/database details | Working on backend |

---

## Development Setup

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Backend runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production API:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
- **Production Frontend:** https://lazygolfing.com

### First-Time Setup

```bash
# Frontend
cd lzg.home && npm ci && npm run build:data

# Backend (on VM)
cd lzg.backend && npm ci
cp .env.example .env  # Configure environment
npx prisma generate
npx prisma migrate dev
npm run start:dev
```

---

## Critical Rules

### Golden Rules (Never Break)

1. **CSV is source of truth** - Never edit JSON in `/data/` directly
2. **Always run `npx prisma generate`** after schema.prisma changes
3. **Protected routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`**
4. **Admin routes need `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`**
5. **All inputs validated** via class-validator DTOs
6. **Never commit secrets** - Check `.env`, API keys, tokens

### Before Completing Tasks

- [ ] Backend: Tests pass (`npm run test`)
- [ ] Frontend: Data check passes (`npm run check:data`)
- [ ] No security issues introduced
- [ ] Code follows existing patterns

### CSV Data Workflow

```bash
# 1. Edit CSV file
vim golfers/golfers.csv

# 2. Regenerate JSON
npm run build:data

# 3. Verify
npm run check:data

# 4. Commit BOTH files
git add golfers/golfers.csv data/golfers.json
git commit -m "data(golfers): update..."
```

---

## Architecture Overview

### Frontend Data Flow

```
CSV files (source of truth)     JSON (build artifact)       Browser
golfers/golfers.csv      -->    data/golfers.json     -->   fetch + render
leagues/leagues.csv      -->    data/leagues.json
tours/tours.csv          -->    data/tours.json
```

### Backend Module Structure

```
src/
├── auth/           # Google OAuth + JWT (cookie: lg_jwt)
├── profile/        # GET/PUT /v1/me
├── rounds/         # CRUD /v1/rounds
├── courses/        # Course management
├── prisma/         # Database service (global module)
└── audit-log/      # Action logging
```

### Database Models

| Model | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `User` | OAuth identity (Google), 1:1 with Player |
| `Player` | Golf profile (handicap, GHIN), has many Rounds |
| `Round` | Golf rounds with stats |
| `Course` | Course data with API integration |
| `RoundLink` | Playing partner linking |
| `Role/UserRole` | RBAC |
| `AuditLog` | Action tracking |

### Authentication Flow

1. Frontend → `GET /auth/google` → Google consent
2. Google → Backend callback with code
3. Backend creates/updates User + Player
4. Backend signs JWT, sets `lg_jwt` HttpOnly cookie
5. Backend redirects to frontend success page
6. Frontend calls `/auth/session` to confirm

---

## Environment Variables

### Backend (.env)

```ini
PORT=3000
NODE_ENV=development|production
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/lazygolfing
JWT_SECRET=<openssl rand -base64 32>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=<from-google-console>
GOOGLE_CALLBACK_URL=http://localhost:3000/auth/google/redirect
```

### CORS Origins (src/main.ts)

```
https://lazygolfing.com
https://www.lazygolfing.com
https://lazygolfing-com.pages.dev
```

For local development, add your origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`).

---

## Troubleshooting Quick Reference

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Table not rendering | JSON not loaded | Check Network tab for 404 |
| Stale data | Browser cache | Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) |
| CSV changes not showing | Forgot build | `npm run build:data` |
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to `main.ts` allowlist |

---

## Documentation Links

### Development Resources

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Master Todo List](./docs/todo/MASTER_TODO.md) | Task tracking with priorities |
| [Technical Debt Register](./docs/technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md) | Code quality issues |
| [API Reference](./docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) | Complete endpoint documentation |
| [Database Schema](./docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma models & ER diagram |

### Architecture Resources

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [System Overview](./docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture |
| [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) | Miami Vice theme & patterns |
| [Competitive Landscape](./docs/research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md) | 11-category market analysis |

### Planning Resources

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Product Roadmap](./docs/plans/PRODUCT_ROADMAP.md) | Vision, timeline, success metrics |
| [Feature Roadmap](./docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list |

### Project-Specific Guides

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [Frontend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) | Next.js 15, React 19, components |
| [Backend CLAUDE.md](./lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | NestJS 11, Prisma, authentication |

---

## AI Working Principles

### Approach

1. **Read first, act later** - Understand existing code before modifying
2. **Follow patterns** - Match the style of surrounding code
3. **Test before completing** - Run tests, verify manually
4. **Security first** - Never expose secrets, validate inputs

### When to Ask

- Requirements are ambiguous
- Security implications unclear
- Breaking changes needed
- Performance trade-offs exist

### When to Proceed

- Implementation matches existing patterns
- Non-breaking additions
- Bug fixes with clear root cause
- Refactoring that preserves behavior

---

## Brand Guidelines

### Voice
- Tagline: "Lazy golf for lazy golfers!" + "Cart Golf Only!"
- Friendly, inclusive, self-aware
- Celebrate small wins and funny losses

### Miami Vice Color Palette

| Color | Hex | Theme Usage |
|-------|-----|-------------|
| Hot Pink | #ff0080 | Dark mode primary |
| Cyan | #00f0ff | Dark mode secondary |
| Purple | #9b59ff | Dark mode accent |
| Coral | #ff6b6b | Light mode primary |
| Orange | #ff7033 | Light mode secondary |
| Yellow | #ffbf00 | Light mode accent |

**Single source of truth:** `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts`

See [Design System](./docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) for full documentation.

---

## Workspace Organization Rules

These rules apply across all repositories in the Lazy Golfing workspace.

### Single Source of Truth Principle

Every piece of data/configuration should have ONE authoritative source:

| What | Source of Truth | Don't Duplicate In |
|------|----------------|-------------------|
| Brand colors | `lzg.home/src/lib/theme.ts` | Individual CSS files |
| SEO metadata | `lzg.home/src/lib/metadata.ts` | Individual page files |
| API types | `lzg.backend/src/` DTOs | Frontend type files |
| CSV data | `public/alpha/*.csv` files | JSON directly |

### Static Asset Organization

All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate folders:

```
public/
├── favicons/     # All favicon files
├── images/       # Logos, OG images, photos
├── patterns/     # SVG patterns, textures
└── fonts/        # Custom fonts (if any)
```

**Never place files loose in public/ root** (except site.webmanifest, robots.txt).

### Social Sharing Standards

| Standard | Specification |
|----------|--------------|
| OG Image Size | 1200x630 pixels (1.91:1 ratio) |
| OG Title | All lowercase for brand ("lazy golfing") |
| OG Description | Tagline: "lazy golf for lazy golfers!" |
| Safe Zone | Center 800x400 area for cropping |

### Documentation Hierarchy

```
/CLAUDE.md                    # Quick reference, critical rules
/docs/                        # Detailed documentation
  ├── plans/                  # Roadmaps, feature lists
  ├── research/               # Market research, competitors
  ├── technical-debt/         # Code quality tracking
  └── architecture/           # System design
/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md          # Frontend-specific rules
/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md       # Backend-specific rules
```

### Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| React Components | PascalCase | `Header.tsx`, `CourseCard.tsx` |
| Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `formatDate.ts` |
| API Routes | kebab-case | `/v1/golf-rounds` |
| CSS Files | kebab-case | `button-styles.css` |
| Assets | kebab-case | `og-image.jpg`, `logo-dark.png` |

---

*For detailed project-specific guidance, see the CLAUDE.md files in each subdirectory.*

# CLAUDE.md - Frontend (lzg.home) > AI assistant guidelines for the Next.js frontend application **Last Updated:** 2025-12-06 **Version:** 2.0 (Next.js migration) --- ## Quick Reference ### Tech Stack - **Framework:** Next.js 15 with React 19 - **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode) - **Styling:** Tailwind CSS 3.4 - **Components:** shadcn/ui (New York style) - **State:** Zustand (when needed) - **Theming:** next-themes (light/dark) ### Environment - **Code editing:** Windows machine - **Backend API (dev):** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40:3000` - **Backend API (prod):** https://api.lazygolfing.com - **Production site:** https://lazygolfing.com (Cloudflare Pages) ### Essential Commands ```bash # Development npm install # Install dependencies npm run dev # Start dev server (localhost:3000) npm run build # Build for production (static export) npm run start # Serve production build locally npm run lint # ESLint checks # Legacy data pipeline (for /alpha site) npm run build:data # CSV → JSON conversion npm run check:data # Verify JSON is current ``` --- ## Project Structure ``` lzg.home/ ├── src/ │ ├── app/ # Next.js App Router │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) │ │ ├── page.tsx # Home page (content only) │ │ ├── globals.css # Global styles + CSS variables │ │ ├── about/page.tsx # About page (uses main layout) │ │ ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal layout) │ │ │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout (no full nav) │ │ │ └── login/page.tsx # Login page │ │ └── [feature]/ # Feature routes │ ├── components/ │ │ ├── layout/ # Layout components (SINGLE SOURCE) │ │ │ ├── Header.tsx # Site header with nav │ │ │ ├── Footer.tsx # Site footer │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── seo/ # SEO components │ │ │ ├── JsonLd.tsx # Schema.org structured data │ │ │ └── index.ts # Barrel export │ │ ├── ui/ # shadcn/ui components │ │ ├── theme-provider.tsx # next-themes wrapper │ │ └── theme-toggle.tsx # Dark/light toggle │ ├── lib/ │ │ ├── utils.ts # cn() utility for class merging │ │ ├── metadata.ts # Centralized SEO metadata config │ │ ├── theme.ts # Brand colors configuration │ │ ├── api.ts # API client (auto-detects env) │ │ └── auth.ts # Authentication utilities │ └── hooks/ # Custom React hooks (future) ├── public/ │ ├── alpha/ # Legacy static site (v1) │ ├── favicons/ # All favicon files (organized) │ ├── images/ # Logo, OG image, photos │ ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns (grid, etc.) │ └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest ├── next.config.ts # Next.js config (static export) ├── tailwind.config.ts # Tailwind + theme colors ├── tsconfig.json # TypeScript config ├── components.json # shadcn/ui config └── package.json # Dependencies & scripts ``` --- ## Layout Architecture (Critical Patterns) ### Single Source of Truth for Header/Footer **RULE:** Header and Footer live ONLY in `layout.tsx` - NEVER duplicate in pages. ```tsx // src/app/layout.tsx - THE pattern import { Header, Footer } from '@/components/layout'; export default function RootLayout({ children }) { return (
{/* Persists across navigation */}
{children}
{/* Persists across navigation */} ); } ``` **Why this matters:** - Header/Footer don't re-render on page navigation (SPA behavior) - Single place to update nav links, branding, footer content - Consistent experience across all pages ### Pages are Content-Only ```tsx // src/app/page.tsx - CORRECT export default function Home() { return ( <>
Hero content...
Features content...
); } // WRONG - Don't do this: export default function Home() { return (
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
Content
{/* NO! This is in layout.tsx */}
); } ``` ### Route Groups for Different Layouts Use parentheses folders `(group-name)` to create route groups with different layouts: ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Main layout (full header/footer) ├── page.tsx # / - uses main layout ├── about/page.tsx # /about - uses main layout ├── (auth)/ # Auth group - different layout │ ├── layout.tsx # Minimal layout (no full nav) │ └── login/page.tsx # /login - uses auth layout └── (dashboard)/ # Future: Dashboard group ├── layout.tsx # Dashboard layout (sidebar) └── rounds/page.tsx # /rounds - uses dashboard layout ``` **Key insight:** The `(auth)` folder name doesn't appear in URLs. `/login` not `/(auth)/login`. --- ## SEO & Metadata (Single Source of Truth) ### Centralized Configuration All SEO metadata is defined in `src/lib/metadata.ts`: ```typescript import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // In any page: export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Rounds', description: 'Track and view your golf rounds', path: '/rounds', }); ``` ### What's Included (Automatic) | Feature | Implementation | Location | |---------|---------------|----------| | **OpenGraph** | og:title, og:description, og:image | `metadata.ts` | | **Twitter Cards** | summary_large_image | `metadata.ts` | | **Schema.org** | WebSite, Organization schemas | `JsonLd` component | | **Favicon** | All sizes, apple-touch-icon | `metadata.ts` | | **Robots** | index, follow, googlebot | `metadata.ts` | | **Canonical URLs** | Per-page via path | `createMetadata()` | ### Schema.org Structured Data ```tsx // In layout.tsx or pages: import { JsonLd, websiteSchema, organizationSchema } from '@/components/seo'; // For dynamic pages (e.g., course details): import { createGolfCourseSchema } from '@/components/seo'; ``` ### Updating SEO Across the Platform | To Change | Edit This File | |-----------|---------------| | Site name, tagline, description | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig` | | OG image | Replace `/public/images/og-image.jpg` (1200x630) | | Favicons | Replace files in `/public/favicons/`, update `metadata.ts` icons config | | PWA manifest | Edit `/public/site.webmanifest` | | Twitter handle | `src/lib/metadata.ts` → `siteConfig.twitterHandle` | | Schema.org data | `src/components/seo/JsonLd.tsx` | | Page-specific meta | Export `metadata` from the page file | --- ## Public Folder Organization (Critical Rules) **RULE:** All static assets MUST be organized in appropriate subfolders. ### Folder Structure ``` public/ ├── favicons/ # All favicon-related files │ ├── favicon.ico # Classic favicon │ ├── favicon.svg # Scalable SVG favicon │ ├── favicon-96x96.png # High-res PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-32x32.png # Standard PNG favicon │ ├── favicon-16x16.png # Small PNG favicon │ ├── apple-touch-icon.png # iOS home screen (180x180) │ ├── web-app-manifest-192x192.png # PWA icon │ └── web-app-manifest-512x512.png # PWA icon large ├── images/ # Photos, logos, OG images │ ├── logo.png │ └── og-image.jpg # 1200x630 for social sharing ├── patterns/ # SVG patterns and textures │ └── grid.svg ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (don't modify) └── site.webmanifest # PWA manifest (references /favicons/) ``` ### Asset Path Rules | Asset Type | Folder | Example Path | |-----------|--------|--------------| | Favicons | `/favicons/` | `/favicons/favicon-32x32.png` | | Logos/Brand | `/images/` | `/images/logo.png` | | OG/Social Images | `/images/` | `/images/og-image.jpg` | | Photos/Screenshots | `/images/` | `/images/screenshot-1.png` | | SVG Patterns | `/patterns/` | `/patterns/grid.svg` | ### Social Sharing Image Specs | Platform | Optimal Size | Notes | |----------|-------------|-------| | OpenGraph (FB, LinkedIn, Discord) | 1200x630 | 1.91:1 ratio | | Twitter Cards | 1200x600 | 2:1 ratio (1200x630 works) | | Keep safe zone | 800x400 center | For cropping variations | ### Never Do This ```tsx // ❌ WRONG - Files loose in public/ src="/logo.png" src="/og-image.jpg" src="/grid.svg" // ✅ CORRECT - Files in organized folders src="/images/logo.png" src="/images/og-image.jpg" src="/patterns/grid.svg" ``` --- ## Miami Vice Theming System ### Centralized Color Configuration All colors are defined in `src/lib/theme.ts`: ```typescript // Full brand palette export const brandColors = { pink: { hex: '#ff0080', hsl: '330 100% 50%' }, cyan: { hex: '#00f0ff', hsl: '184 100% 50%' }, purple: { hex: '#9b59ff', hsl: '270 70% 60%' }, orange: { hex: '#ff7033', hsl: '25 100% 55%' }, yellow: { hex: '#ffbf00', hsl: '45 100% 55%' }, coral: { hex: '#ff6b6b', hsl: '10 100% 65%' }, }; ``` ### Theme-Adaptive Colors (globals.css) ```css :root { /* Light theme uses warmer colors */ --brand-primary: 25 100% 55%; /* Orange */ --brand-secondary: 45 100% 55%; /* Yellow */ --brand-accent: 10 100% 65%; /* Coral */ } .dark { /* Dark theme uses neon colors */ --brand-primary: 330 100% 55%; /* Hot Pink */ --brand-secondary: 184 100% 50%; /* Cyan */ --brand-accent: 270 70% 60%; /* Purple */ } ``` ### Using Theme Colors ```tsx // Theme-adaptive (changes with light/dark):
// Uses --brand-primary → --brand-secondary // Static brand colors (always same): // #ff0080 // #00f0ff
// Pink → Cyan
// Orange → Coral → Pink // Utility classes:
// Text glow effect
// Glass morphism
// Background glow effects ``` ### Theme Toggle ```tsx import { ThemeToggle } from '@/components/theme-toggle'; // In header: // Renders sun/moon icon button ``` --- ## Adding shadcn/ui Components ```bash # Add a component npx shadcn-ui@latest add button npx shadcn-ui@latest add card npx shadcn-ui@latest add dialog # Components go to src/components/ui/ ``` ### Available Components (pre-installed) - `Button` - Primary UI button - Additional components added as needed ### Usage Pattern ```tsx import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; ``` --- ## API Integration ### API Client (`src/lib/api.ts`) ```typescript import { apiRequest, API_BASE, IS_DEV } from '@/lib/api'; // GET request const { ok, data } = await apiRequest('/v1/endpoint'); // POST with body const result = await apiRequest('/v1/rounds', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ courseName: 'Test', strokes: 85 }), }); // Response type interface ApiResponse { ok: boolean; status: number; data: T | null; } ``` **Features:** - Auto-detects localhost vs production - Includes credentials (for JWT cookies) - CSRF token extraction from cookies - Handles 204 No Content responses ### Authentication (`src/lib/auth.ts`) ```typescript import { getSession, getMe, logout, getGoogleLoginUrl } from '@/lib/auth'; // Check auth status const session = await getSession(); if (session.authenticated) { console.log(session.user.email); } // Get user profile const { ok, data: profile } = await getMe(); // Redirect to Google OAuth window.location.href = getGoogleLoginUrl(); ``` ### Environment Variables | Variable | Purpose | Default | |----------|---------|---------| | `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` | Backend API URL | Auto-detects based on hostname | **Auto-detection logic:** - If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` is set → use that - If hostname is `localhost` or `127.0.0.1` → use `http://localhost:3000` - Otherwise → use `https://api.lazygolfing.com` **Development `.env.local`:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.40:3000 ``` --- ## Routing ### App Router Structure ``` src/app/ ├── layout.tsx # Root layout (Header, Footer, Schema) ├── page.tsx # / (home) - content only ├── about/page.tsx # /about ├── (auth)/ # Auth route group (minimal header) │ ├── layout.tsx # Auth-specific layout │ └── login/page.tsx # /login ├── (main)/ # Future: Pages with full nav │ ├── rounds/ │ │ ├── page.tsx # /rounds (list) │ │ └── [id]/page.tsx # /rounds/123 (detail) │ ├── leagues/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /leagues │ ├── tournaments/ │ │ └── page.tsx # /tournaments │ └── profile/ │ └── page.tsx # /profile ``` ### Route Groups Explained | Group | Layout | Use Case | |-------|--------|----------| | `(auth)` | Minimal header, no nav | Login, signup, forgot password | | `(main)` | Full header/footer | Most public pages | | `(dashboard)` | Sidebar + header | Authenticated user pages | ### Link to Legacy Site ```tsx // Link to old pages during migration: Sign In (Legacy) ``` --- ## Cloudflare Pages Deployment ### Build Settings - **Build command:** `npm run build` - **Build output directory:** `out` - **Root directory:** `lzg.home` ### Static Export The `next.config.ts` is configured for static export: ```typescript const nextConfig = { output: 'export', // Static HTML export trailingSlash: true, // /about/ instead of /about images: { unoptimized: true }, // Required for static }; ``` ### Output Structure ``` out/ ├── index.html # New home page ├── rounds/index.html # New rounds page ├── alpha/ # Legacy site (copied from public/) │ ├── index.html │ └── ... └── _next/ # JS/CSS bundles ``` --- ## Development Workflow ### Starting Development ```bash cd lzg.home npm install npm run dev ``` ### Creating a New Page ```tsx // src/app/courses/page.tsx import { createMetadata } from '@/lib/metadata'; // 1. Add page-specific metadata export const metadata = createMetadata({ title: 'Courses', description: 'Browse golf courses and view ratings, slopes, and reviews.', path: '/courses', }); // 2. Page is content-only (Header/Footer come from layout.tsx) export default function CoursesPage() { return (

Courses

{/* Content here */}
); } ``` **Checklist for new pages:** - [ ] Export `metadata` using `createMetadata()` for SEO - [ ] Don't include Header/Footer (they're in layout.tsx) - [ ] Use the standard content wrapper pattern - [ ] Add to navigation in `components/layout/Header.tsx` if needed ### Creating a Component ```tsx // src/components/course-card.tsx interface CourseCardProps { name: string; location: string; par: number; } export function CourseCard({ name, location, par }: CourseCardProps) { return (

{name}

{location}

Par {par}

); } ``` --- ## Styling Guidelines ### Use Tailwind Classes ```tsx // Good - Tailwind utility classes
// Avoid - inline styles
``` ### CSS Variables for Theming ```tsx // Good - uses theme variables
// Avoid - hardcoded colors
``` ### Responsive Design ```tsx // Mobile-first approach
``` --- ## Legacy Site (Alpha) The previous static site is preserved at `/alpha/`: - **URL:** https://lazygolfing.com/alpha/ - **Location:** `public/alpha/` - **Purpose:** Fallback during migration ### Legacy Files ``` public/alpha/ ├── index.html, profile.html, rounds.html, ... ├── js/auth.js # Authentication client ├── js/profile.js, rounds.js # Page logic ├── css/style.css # Custom styles ├── data/ # Generated JSON ├── golfers/, leagues/, tours/ # CSV + scripts └── scripts/convert-csv.js # Data pipeline ``` ### Maintaining Legacy Data The CSV → JSON pipeline still works: ```bash npm run build:data # Converts CSVs in public/alpha/ npm run check:data # Verifies JSON is current ``` --- ## File Naming Conventions | Type | Convention | Example | |------|------------|---------| | Components | PascalCase | `CourseCard.tsx` | | Pages | kebab-case folders | `src/app/my-rounds/page.tsx` | | Utilities | camelCase | `utils.ts`, `apiClient.ts` | | Hooks | use prefix | `useAuth.ts`, `useRounds.ts` | | Types | PascalCase | `types.ts` → `export type Round` | --- ## Common Patterns ### Client Components ```tsx 'use client'; // Add at top for interactivity import { useState } from 'react'; export function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); return ; } ``` ### Server Components (Default) ```tsx // No 'use client' - runs on server export default async function Page() { const data = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data'); return
{/* render data */}
; } ``` ### Loading States ```tsx // src/app/rounds/loading.tsx export default function Loading() { return
Loading...
; } ``` --- ## Debugging ### Check Theme ```javascript // Browser console document.documentElement.classList.contains('dark') // true if dark mode ``` ### Check API Base ```javascript // Environment variable in browser console.log(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL); ``` ### Common Issues | Symptom | Cause | Solution | |---------|-------|----------| | Hydration mismatch | Theme flash | `suppressHydrationWarning` on html | | 404 on routes | Static export | Check `trailingSlash: true` | | Styles not loading | Tailwind purge | Check `content` in tailwind.config | | API CORS errors | Wrong origin | Add localhost to backend CORS | | Build fails | Type errors | Run `npm run lint` first | --- ## Related Documentation | Document | Description | |----------|-------------| | [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) | Root monorepo overview | | [/lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.backend/CLAUDE.md) | Backend API documentation | | [/docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md](../docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) | Complete API endpoint reference | | [/docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md](../docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma schema & ER diagram | | [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture | | [/docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md](../docs/design/DESIGN_INSPIRATION.md) | Miami Vice design system | | [/docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md](../docs/plans/FEATURE_ROADMAP.md) | Comprehensive feature list | --- *This file was updated during the Next.js v2.0 + Miami Vice migration (2025-12-06).* *Architecture patterns (Layout, SEO, Route Groups) added 2025-12-06.*

1→# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)
     2→
     3→> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend
     4→
     5→**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## Quick Reference
    10→
    11→### Environment
    12→
    13→- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
    14→- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
    15→- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com
    16→
    17→### Essential Commands (Run on VM)
    18→
    19→```bash
    20→# Development
    21→npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
    22→npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
    23→npm run test                    # Unit tests
    24→npm run lint                    # ESLint
    25→
    26→# Database
    27→npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
    28→npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
    29→npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555
    30→
    31→# Production
    32→pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
    33→pm2 restart lg-api              # After deploy
    34→```
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Module Structure
    39→
    40→```
    41→src/
    42→├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
    43→├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
    44→├── config/
    45→│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
    46→├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
    47→│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
    48→│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
    49→│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
    50→│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
    51→│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
    52→│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
    53→│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
    54→│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
    55→│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
    56→├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
    57→│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
    58→│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
    59→│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
    60→├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
    61→│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
    62→│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
    63→│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
    64→├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
    65→│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
    66→│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
    67→│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
    68→├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
    69→│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
    70→└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    71→    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    72→    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
    73→```
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## API Endpoints
    78→
    79→### Authentication (Public)
    80→
    81→| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
    82→|--------|------|-------|-------------|
    83→| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
    84→| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
    85→| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
    86→| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |
    87→
    88→### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)
    89→
    90→| Method | Path | Description |
    91→|--------|------|-------------|
    92→| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
    93→| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
    94→| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
    95→| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
    96→| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
    97→| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |
    98→
    99→### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)
   100→
   101→| Method | Path | Description |
   102→|--------|------|-------------|
   103→| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
   104→| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
   105→| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
   106→| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
   107→| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
   108→| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
   109→| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
   110→| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |
   111→
   112→### Courses
   113→
   114→| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
   115→|--------|------|-------|-------------|
   116→| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
   117→| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
   118→| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
   119→| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
   120→| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
   121→| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
   122→| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
   123→| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## Database Schema
   128→
   129→### Model Relationships
   130→
   131→```
   132→User (OAuth identity)
   133→  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
   134→           └── 1:N Round
   135→                     └── N:1 Course (optional)
   136→
   137→Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
   138→AuditLog (standalone)
   139→```
   140→
   141→### Key Fields
   142→
   143→| Model | Key Fields |
   144→|-------|------------|
   145→| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
   146→| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
   147→| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
   148→| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Authentication Flow
   153→
   154→```
   155→1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
   156→2. Redirect to Google consent
   157→3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
   158→4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
   159→5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
   160→6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
   161→7. Redirect to frontend success page
   162→```
   163→
   164→### Cookie Config
   165→
   166→```typescript
   167→{
   168→  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
   169→  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
   170→  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
   171→  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
   172→}
   173→```
   174→
   175→### Protecting Routes
   176→
   177→```typescript
   178→// Basic JWT protection
   179→@Get('protected-route')
   180→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
   181→async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
   182→  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
   183→}
   184→
   185→// Admin-only route
   186→@Put('admin-route')
   187→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
   188→async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
   189→  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
   190→}
   191→```
   192→
   193→---
   194→
   195→## CSRF Protection
   196→
   197→### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern
   198→
   199→Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.
   200→
   201→**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
   202→**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
   203→**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)
   204→
   205→```typescript
   206→// Frontend request example
   207→fetch('/v1/me', {
   208→  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
   209→  headers: {
   210→    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
   211→  }
   212→});
   213→```
   214→
   215→**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests
   216→
   217→---
   218→
   219→## Rate Limiting
   220→
   221→Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:
   222→
   223→| Category | Limit | Window |
   224→|----------|-------|--------|
   225→| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
   226→| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
   227→| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |
   228→
   229→**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→## Adding a New Endpoint
   234→
   235→```typescript
   236→// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
   237→export class CreateThingDto {
   238→  @IsString()
   239→  @IsNotEmpty()
   240→  name: string;
   241→}
   242→
   243→// 2. Add service method
   244→async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
   245→  return this.prisma.thing.create({
   246→    data: { ...dto, playerId },
   247→  });
   248→}
   249→
   250→// 3. Add controller route
   251→@Post()
   252→@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
   253→async create(
   254→  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
   255→  @CurrentUser() user: any,
   256→) {
   257→  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
   258→  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
   259→}
   260→
   261→// 4. Write tests and run
   262→npm run test
   263→```

   392→### Common Issues
