# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)

> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.

### Backend Priority Applications

| Priority | Backend Implementation |
|----------|----------------------|
| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |

### Backend Checklist (Every Change)

- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
- [ ] Sensitive data filtered from responses
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
- [ ] Database queries are efficient (check for N+1)
- [ ] `npm run test` passes
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes

### Security Non-Negotiables

```typescript
// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
  // Validate ownership in service layer
}

// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
@Delete(':id')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async delete(@Param('id') id: string) { }

// ❌ NEVER: Unprotected mutation
@Post()
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }  // NO GUARDS = SECURITY HOLE
```

---

## Quick Reference

### Environment

- **Code editing:** Windows machine (this repo)
- **Runtime:** Ubuntu VM at `192.168.1.40`
- **Production:** https://api.lazygolfing.com

### Essential Commands (Run on VM)

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev               # Hot-reload server
npm run start:debug             # Debug mode (port 9229)
npm run test                    # Unit tests
npm run lint                    # ESLint

# Database
npx prisma generate             # REQUIRED after schema changes
npx prisma migrate dev --name <name>  # Create migration
npx prisma studio               # GUI at 192.168.1.40:5555

# Production
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 100     # View logs
pm2 restart lg-api              # After deploy
```

---

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
├── config/
│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
```

---

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |
| POST | `/auth/logout` | CSRF | Clear session |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

---

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
           └── 1:N Round
                     └── N:1 Course (optional)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

---

## Adding a New Endpoint

```typescript
// 1. Add DTO (src/[module]/dto/[action].dto.ts)
export class CreateThingDto {
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  name: string;
}

// 2. Add service method
async createThing(playerId: string, dto: CreateThingDto) {
  return this.prisma.thing.create({
    data: { ...dto, playerId },
  });
}

// 3. Add controller route
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)
async create(
  @Body() dto: CreateThingDto,
  @CurrentUser() user: any,
) {
  const profile = await this.profileService.getProfile(user.id);
  return this.thingService.createThing(profile.player.id, dto);
}

// 4. Write tests and run
```
```bash
npm run test
```

### Common Issues

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| Migration failed | DB not running | Check PostgreSQL status |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Check cookie, re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to main.ts allowlist |
| 500 Internal Server Error | Uncaught exception | Check PM2 logs |

# CLAUDE.md - Backend (lzg.backend)

> AI assistant guidelines for the NestJS API backend

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06

---

## ⚠️ Critical Priorities (Backend API)

> **See [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) for the complete priority hierarchy.** This section covers backend-specific applications of those principles.

### Backend Priority Applications

| Priority | Backend Implementation |
|----------|----------------------|
| 🔒 **Security** | JWT + CSRF on all mutations, input validation via DTOs, parameterized queries (Prisma), rate limiting, never log secrets, RBAC with AdminGuard |
| 🏗️ **Foundational** | Prisma as single DB interface, NestJS module structure, consistent error responses, proper database constraints/indexes |
| 🛡️ **Stability** | Try-catch on all async, transaction rollbacks, connection pooling, graceful shutdown, health checks |
| ✨ **UX/UI** | Fast API responses (<200ms), meaningful error messages, proper HTTP status codes, pagination on lists |
| 🎯 **Lean** | No unused endpoints, minimal dependencies, efficient queries (avoid N+1), lazy load relations |
| 🧹 **Clean** | Service/Controller separation, DTOs for all inputs, consistent naming, typed Prisma queries |
| ⚡ **Performance** | Database indexes on query fields, query optimization, connection pooling, response caching where safe |
| 📊 **Observable** | Audit logging for mutations, structured error logging, PM2 monitoring |

### Backend Checklist (Every Change)

**Quick checklist** (see `@../docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` for detailed checklists):

- [ ] Protected routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)`
- [ ] Admin routes have `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)`
- [ ] All inputs validated via class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data (no IDOR)
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs
- [ ] `npm run test` passes
- [ ] `npx prisma generate` run after schema changes

### Security Non-Negotiables

```typescript
// ✅ ALWAYS: Protected mutation endpoint
@Post()
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto, @CurrentUser() user: User) {
  // Validate ownership in service layer
}

// ✅ ALWAYS: Admin-only endpoint
@Delete(':id')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async delete(@Param('id') id: string) { }

// ❌ NEVER: Unprotected mutation
@Post()
async create(@Body() dto: CreateDto) { }  // NO GUARDS = SECURITY HOLE
```

## Module Structure

```
src/
├── main.ts                     # Bootstrap (CORS, Helmet, validation, cookies)
├── app.module.ts               # Root module + ThrottlerModule
├── config/
│   └── env.validation.ts       # Environment variable validation
├── auth/                       # Google OAuth + JWT + CSRF
│   ├── auth.controller.ts      # /auth/google, /auth/session, /auth/logout
│   ├── auth.service.ts         # findOrCreateUser, validateUser, ensureRoles
│   ├── google.strategy.ts      # Passport Google OAuth
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts         # Passport JWT validation
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts       # Guard for protected routes
│   ├── admin.guard.ts          # Guard for admin-only routes
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts           # CSRF double-submit protection
│   ├── csrf.util.ts            # CSRF token generation
│   └── current-user.decorator.ts  # @CurrentUser() decorator
├── profile/                    # User profile + stats
│   ├── profile.controller.ts   # GET/PUT /v1/me, /v1/me/stats, /v1/me/export
│   ├── profile.service.ts      # getProfile, updateProfile, getStats, export
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts   # Validation DTO
├── rounds/                     # Golf rounds + linking
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts    # CRUD /v1/rounds + link endpoints
│   ├── rounds.service.ts       # Business logic + partner matching
│   └── dto/                    # create-round.dto.ts, update-round.dto.ts, import-csv.dto.ts
├── courses/                    # Course management + Golf API
│   ├── courses.controller.ts   # Search, nearby, CRUD
│   ├── courses.service.ts      # Search, API integration, Haversine
│   └── dto/                    # create-course.dto.ts, update-course.dto.ts
├── prisma/                     # Database (Global module)
│   └── prisma.service.ts       # Extends PrismaClient
└── audit-log/                  # Action logging
    ├── audit-log.module.ts     # Module definition
    └── audit-log.service.ts    # log(), getUserLogs(), cleanup()
```

## API Endpoints

### Authentication (Public)

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/auth/google` | Google OAuth | Start OAuth flow |
| GET | `/auth/google/redirect` | Google OAuth | OAuth callback |
| GET | `/auth/session` | None | Check login status |

### Profile (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/me` | Get user + player profile |
| PUT | `/v1/me` | Update player profile |
| GET | `/v1/me/stats` | Get user statistics |
| GET | `/v1/me/playing-partners` | Get aggregated partners |
| GET | `/v1/me/export` | GDPR data export |
| DELETE | `/v1/me` | Delete account (cascade) |

### Rounds (Requires JWT + CSRF)

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/rounds` | List player's rounds (paginated) |
| POST | `/v1/rounds` | Create round |
| PUT | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Update round |
| DELETE | `/v1/rounds/:id` | Soft delete round |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/import-csv` | Bulk import rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/suggestions` | Find playing partner matches |
| POST | `/v1/rounds/:id/link/:targetId` | Link two rounds |
| GET | `/v1/rounds/:id/linked` | Get linked rounds |

### Courses

| Method | Path | Guard | Description |
|--------|------|-------|-------------|
| GET | `/v1/courses/search` | None | Search by name (+ API) |
| GET | `/v1/courses/nearby` | None | GPS-based search |
| GET | `/v1/courses` | None | List all courses |
| GET | `/v1/courses/:id` | None | Course details + stats |
| POST | `/v1/courses` | JWT+CSRF | Create course |
| POST | `/v1/courses/import` | JWT+CSRF | Import from Golf API |
| PUT | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Update course |
| DELETE | `/v1/courses/:id` | JWT+CSRF+Admin | Delete course |

## Database Schema

### Model Relationships

```
User (OAuth identity)
  └── 1:1 Player (golf profile)
           └── 1:N Round
                     └── N:1 Course (optional)

Role ←─ N:N ─→ User (via UserRole)
AuditLog (standalone)
```

### Key Fields

| Model | Key Fields |
|-------|------------|
| User | email, provider, providerId, avatarUrl, lastLoginAt |
| Player | firstName, lastName, ghinNumber, handicap |
| Round | strokes, front9, back9, girPct, firPct, totalPutts, birdies, pars, bogeys |
| Course | name, city, state, totalPar, courseRating, slopeRating, apiCourseId |

---

## Authentication Flow

```
1. Frontend → GET /auth/google
2. Redirect to Google consent
3. Google → /auth/google/redirect with code
4. GoogleStrategy exchanges code for user info
5. auth.service.findOrCreateUser() → User + Player
6. Sign JWT, set lg_jwt cookie (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=None, 7 days)
7. Redirect to frontend success page
```

### Cookie Config

```typescript
{
  httpOnly: true,        // XSS protection
  secure: true,          // HTTPS only (production)
  sameSite: 'none',      // Cross-origin allowed
  maxAge: 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000,  // 7 days
}
```

### Protecting Routes

```typescript
// Basic JWT protection
@Get('protected-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard)
async myRoute(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // user contains JWT payload: { sub: userId, email }
}

// Admin-only route
@Put('admin-route')
@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, CsrfGuard, AdminGuard)
async adminOnly(@CurrentUser() user: any) {
  // Only users with 'admin' role can access
}
```

---

## CSRF Protection

### Double-Submit Cookie Pattern

Protected endpoints require the `X-CSRF-Token` header to match the `csrf_token` cookie.

**Cookie:** Set at login alongside `lg_jwt`
**Header:** `X-CSRF-Token: <token-value>`
**Validation:** Constant-time comparison (prevents timing attacks)

```typescript
// Frontend request example
fetch('/v1/me', {
  credentials: 'include',  // Include cookies
  headers: {
    'X-CSRF-Token': getCookie('csrf_token')
  }
});
```

**Skip for:** GET, HEAD, OPTIONS requests

---

## Rate Limiting

Global rate limiting via `@nestjs/throttler`:

| Category | Limit | Window |
|----------|-------|--------|
| Default | 120 requests | 1 minute |
| Auth endpoints | 20 requests | 1 minute |
| Session check | 20 requests | 1 minute |

**Response when exceeded:** `429 Too Many Requests`

```bash
curl https://api.lazygolfing.com/auth/session
```

### Common Issues

| Symptom | Cause | Solution |
|---------|-------|----------|
| `Prisma Client not found` | Schema changed | `npx prisma generate` |
| Migration failed | DB not running | Check PostgreSQL status |
| 401 Unauthorized | Invalid JWT | Check cookie, re-login |
| CORS error | Origin not allowed | Add to main.ts allowlist |
| 500 Internal Server Error | Uncaught exception | Check PM2 logs |

---

## Deployment

```bash
# 1. SSH to VM
# 2. Pull latest code
git pull origin main

# 3. Install dependencies
npm ci

# 4. Run migrations
npx prisma migrate deploy

# 5. Rebuild
npm run build

# 6. Restart PM2
pm2 restart lg-api

# 7. Verify
curl http://localhost:3000/
pm2 logs lg-api --lines 20
```

### PM2 Config (ecosystem.config.js)

```javascript
{
  name: 'lg-api',
  script: 'dist/src/main.js',
  instances: 1,
  exec_mode: 'fork',
  autorestart: true,
  max_restarts: 25,
  max_memory_restart: '300M',
}
```

---

## Security Checklist

Before deploying new endpoints:
- [ ] Uses `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard)` if protected
- [ ] Input validated with class-validator DTOs
- [ ] User can only access their own data
- [ ] Sensitive data filtered from responses
- [ ] No secrets in code or logs

---

## Related Documentation

| Document | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| [/CLAUDE.md](../CLAUDE.md) | Root monorepo overview |
| [/lzg.home/CLAUDE.md](../lzg.home/CLAUDE.md) | Frontend documentation |
| [/docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md](../docs/architecture/API_REFERENCE.md) | Complete API endpoint reference |
| [/docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md](../docs/architecture/DATABASE_SCHEMA.md) | Prisma schema & ER diagram |
| [/docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md](../docs/architecture/SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md) | High-level architecture |

---

*This documentation covers the NestJS 11 backend for the Lazy Golfing platform.*

All done! Here's a comprehensive summary of the CLAUDE.md optimization:

## CLAUDE.md Optimization Complete

### New Structure Overview

```
Lazy Golfing/
├── CLAUDE.md                              # Root (525 lines) - with TOC + auto-discovery table
├── docs/
│   └── guidelines/
│       ├── COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md       # NEW: Consolidated all checklists (145 lines)
│       └── NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md          # NEW: Complete naming guide (175 lines)
├── lzg.home/
│   ├── CLAUDE.md                          # Frontend (755 lines) - with TOC
│   └── src/components/
│       └── CLAUDE.md                      # NEW: Component patterns (165 lines)
└── lzg.backend/
    ├── CLAUDE.md                          # Backend (545 lines) - with TOC
    └── src/auth/
        └── CLAUDE.md                      # NEW: Auth module patterns (185 lines)
```

### Key Improvements

| Improvement | Benefit |
|-------------|---------|
| **Table of Contents** | Easy navigation in 400+ line files |
| **Auto-Discovery Table** | Clear explanation of which CLAUDE.md loads when |
| **External Checklist Reference** | Single source of truth for all checklists via `@import` |
| **Folder-Specific CLAUDE.md** | Context loads only when working in that directory |
| **Naming Conventions Doc** | Complete reference for all naming patterns |

### New Files Created

1. **`docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md`**
   - Universal checklist (all changes)
   - Frontend-specific checklist (component, page)
   - Backend-specific checklist (endpoint, database)
   - Pre-commit and pre-deploy checklists

2. **`docs/guidelines/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md`**
   - File naming (components, pages, utilities)
   - Code naming (frontend, backend, database)
   - API naming (endpoints, HTTP methods, fields)
   - Git naming (branches, commit messages)

3. **`lzg.home/src/components/CLAUDE.md`**
   - Layout components rule (Header/Footer in layout only)
   - shadcn/ui rules (never edit `ui/` directly)
   - Component patterns (file structure, styling)
   - Size limits (<200 lines)

4. **`lzg.backend/src/auth/CLAUDE.md`**
   - Auth flow diagram
   - Guard stack order
   - Cookie configuration
   - CSRF protection details
   - Common mistakes to avoid

### Claude Code Discovery Behavior

Claude Code now loads context hierarchically:

- **Working in `/lzg.home/src/components/`**: Loads 3 CLAUDE.md files (root → frontend → components)
- **Working in `/lzg.backend/src/auth/`**: Loads 3 CLAUDE.md files (root → backend → auth)
- **Working elsewhere**: Loads appropriate subset

### Commits Pushed

- `lzg.home` → `a9d2177` - TOC, checklist refs, components/CLAUDE.md
- `lzg.backend` → `d8fc342` - TOC, checklist refs, auth/CLAUDE.md

### Files Not Git-Tracked (Root Level)

These files exist but aren't in a git repo:
- `/CLAUDE.md` (updated with TOC + auto-discovery table)
- `/docs/guidelines/COMPLETION_CHECKLISTS.md` (new)
- `/docs/guidelines/NAMING_CONVENTIONS.md` (new)

The workspace is now optimized for foundational development! Claude Code will automatically load the right context based on which directory you're working in.

content.text:
# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes your task and automatically routes to the optimal execution strategy per CLAUDE.md's Intelligent Prompt Routing system.

## Routing Logic

**If no task description provided:**
- Run `/check-todos` to list outstanding work
- Await user selection

**If task description provided, analyze and route:**

### 1. Planning Tasks → `/create-plan`
**Keywords:** "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase", "roadmap"
**Complexity:** Multi-phase features, complex architecture
**Example:** "Plan the analytics dashboard feature"

→ Invoke: `/create-plan {{task_description}}`

### 2. Decision-Making → `/consider:*` frameworks
**Keywords:** "should we", "which approach", "evaluate", "compare", "decide"
**Examples:**
- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`

→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework

### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"

→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description

### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
**Criteria:**
- 30min - 2hr estimated work
- 3-10 files to modify
- Clear scope but needs fresh context
**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"

→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`

### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"

→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`

### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
**Criteria:**
- 1-2 files
- <30min work
- Clear, straightforward
**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"

→ Execute directly in current context

## Implementation

**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
```javascript
const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();

if (!taskDescription) {
  // No task provided - check todos
  return "/check-todos";
}
```

**Step 2: Analyze Task**

Examine task description for:
- **Keywords** indicating task type
- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)

**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**

Based on analysis:

```javascript
// Planning tasks
if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
}

// Decision-making tasks
else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
}

// Debugging tasks
else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
}

// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
  // Offer to run after creation
}

// Multi-stage features
else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
}

// Simple/direct - execute in current context
else {
  executeDirectly();
}
```

**Step 4: Follow Through**

After routing:
- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
- If framework: present analysis and next steps

## Examples

```bash
# Automatically routes to /create-plan
/start-task Plan the user authentication system

# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?

# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure

# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
/start-task Refactor the calendar view components

# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach

# Direct execution
/start-task Fix typo in README.md

# Check todos (no argument)
/start-task
```

## Notes

- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed


ARGUMENTS: Perfect! If helpful should we take it even further? Or is it as good as it can be?

Good question! Let me evaluate objectively based on best practices and diminishing returns.

## Current State Assessment

| Aspect | Status | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Hierarchy** | ✅ Optimal | 3 levels (root → project → module) is the recommended max |
| **File sizes** | ✅ Good | All under 800 lines, well within 10k word limit |
| **Navigation** | ✅ Added | TOCs in all main files |
| **Auto-discovery** | ✅ Documented | Clear table showing what loads when |
| **External refs** | ✅ Implemented | Checklists and naming conventions extracted |
| **Folder-specific** | ✅ Created | Components + Auth modules have their own context |

## Could We Go Further? Yes, But...

| Potential Addition | Benefit | Concern |
|-------------------|---------|---------|
| More folder CLAUDE.md files (`rounds/`, `courses/`, `profile/`) | Hyper-specific context | **Overkill** - these modules are simple, patterns already covered |
| `.claude/settings.json` project defaults | Pre-configured model/tools | Already have hooks configured |
| Split root CLAUDE.md into imports | Smaller main file | **Fragmentation** - harder to read as a whole |
| Add `prisma/CLAUDE.md` | Database-specific rules | Prisma workflow already well-documented in backend CLAUDE.md |

## My Honest Assessment

**The current structure is at the "Goldilocks zone"** - not too sparse, not over-engineered.

### What makes it "good enough":

1. **Claude Code discovery works well with 2-3 levels** - more creates confusion
2. **Each file has a clear purpose** - no overlap or redundancy
3. **External docs use `@import`** - checklists/naming are reusable
4. **Folder-specific files exist where patterns are complex** (auth, components)

### What would be diminishing returns:

- Adding CLAUDE.md to every folder = maintenance burden
- Over-fragmenting = harder to maintain single source of truth
- More docs = more to keep updated

## Recommendation

**Stop here for CLAUDE.md structure.** It's foundationally solid.

**Better use of time now:**
1. Start building features on this foundation
2. Add folder-specific CLAUDE.md files **only when you encounter repeated confusion** in a specific area
3. Focus on the actual platform code

The documentation will naturally evolve as you build - that's when you'll discover which areas truly need more context.

---

**TL;DR:** It's as good as it should be right now. Further optimization would be premature. Build features, and let the documentation grow organically where needed.

Ready to start on actual platform development?

# Master Todo List

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Status:** Active

---

## Priority Legend
- **P0:** Critical - Must do immediately
- **P1:** High - Do within 1-2 weeks
- **P2:** Medium - Do within 1 month
- **P3:** Low - Do when time permits

---

## Backend Tasks

### P0 - Critical

- [ ] **Enable TypeScript strict mode**
  - Location: `lzg.backend/tsconfig.json`
  - Impact: Better type safety, catch bugs earlier
  - Effort: 2-3 days (fix ~50-100 type errors)

- [ ] **Add rate limiting**
  - Install `@nestjs/throttler`
  - Configure: 100 req/15min general, 10 req/15min auth
  - Protect against abuse/DoS

- [ ] **Add database indexes**
  - `rounds.date` (sorting)
  - `rounds.courseName` (search)
  - Create migration

### P1 - High Priority

- [ ] **Write unit tests**
  - RoundsService CRUD operations
  - ProfileService stats calculation
  - AuthService JWT generation
  - Target: 60% coverage

- [ ] **Standardize error handling**
  - Create centralized exception filter
  - Define error response format
  - Document all error codes

- [ ] **Add health check endpoint**
  - `GET /health`
  - Return: status, uptime, database connectivity
  - Enable monitoring integration

- [ ] **Add pagination**
  - `GET /v1/rounds` with `page`, `limit` params
  - Return metadata: total, totalPages

### P2 - Medium Priority

- [ ] **Add input sanitization**
  - Prevent XSS in text fields
  - Strip HTML from courseName/notes

- [ ] **Configure soft delete cleanup**
  - Cron job to purge records > 30 days
  - Log deletions for audit trail

- [ ] **Create audit log query endpoint**
  - `GET /admin/audit-logs`
  - Requires admin role
  - Support filters: userId, action, dateRange

- [ ] **Add API documentation**
  - Install `@nestjs/swagger`
  - Document all endpoints
  - Generate OpenAPI spec

### P3 - Low Priority

- [ ] **Extract constants/magic values**
  - JWT expiry, limits, CORS origins
  - Move to constants.ts or env vars

- [ ] **Standardize logging**
  - Remove console.log
  - Use NestJS Logger everywhere
  - Configure log levels

---

## Frontend Tasks

### P0 - Critical

- [ ] **Add loading states**
  - Skeleton screens for lists
  - Progress indicators for forms
  - "Saving..." on buttons

- [ ] **Add error messages**
  - Toast notifications for failures
  - User-friendly error text
  - Retry options

- [ ] **Add delete confirmation**
  - "Are you sure?" modal
  - Two-step delete process

### P1 - High Priority

- [ ] **Add data export**
  - "Export Data" button in profile
  - JSON and CSV formats
  - Include all user data

- [ ] **Optimize images**
  - Compress existing images
  - Add lazy loading
  - Consider WebP format

- [ ] **Clear browser console warnings**
  - Fix missing alt attributes
  - Update deprecated jQuery methods

### P2 - Medium Priority

- [ ] **Minify CSS/JS**
  - Set up build process
  - Generate source maps
  - Configure for Cloudflare Pages

- [ ] **Add keyboard shortcuts**
  - N: New round
  - /: Focus search
  - Esc: Close modal

- [ ] **Improve form UX**
  - Add "Today" button to date picker
  - Course autocomplete
  - Unsaved changes warning

### P3 - Low Priority

- [ ] **Refactor long functions**
  - renderRounds (>100 lines)
  - loadStats
  - Target: max 50 lines

- [ ] **Extract reusable components**
  - Modal wrapper
  - Toast notification
  - Card component

---

## Infrastructure Tasks

### P1 - High Priority

- [ ] **Set up CI/CD pipeline**
  - GitHub Actions
  - Auto-test on PR
  - Auto-deploy on merge

- [ ] **Configure automated backups**
  - Daily pg_dump
  - Retain 30 days
  - Off-site storage (R2/S3)

- [ ] **Set up error monitoring**
  - Sentry or similar
  - Alert on error rate > 1%

- [ ] **Create staging environment**
  - Separate database
  - staging.api.lazygolfing.com

### P2 - Medium Priority

- [ ] **Add uptime monitoring**
  - UptimeRobot or similar
  - Alert on downtime > 1 min

- [ ] **Set up log aggregation**
  - Papertrail or similar
  - Searchable logs

- [ ] **Add Content Security Policy**
  - Configure CSP headers
  - Start in report-only mode

---

## Documentation Tasks

### P1 - High Priority

- [ ] **Create user guide**
  - FAQ page
  - How-to articles
  - Tooltips for features

- [ ] **Document all APIs**
  - Swagger/OpenAPI
  - Example requests/responses

### P2 - Medium Priority

- [ ] **Create architecture diagrams**
  - System overview
  - Database ERD
  - Auth flow diagram

- [ ] **Write runbook**
  - "Backend is down" procedure
  - "Database issues" procedure
  - Contact escalation

---

## Quick Wins

Low-effort, high-impact tasks that can be done anytime:

- [ ] Add "Copy round" feature
- [ ] Add bulk delete (multi-select)
- [ ] Add round count badge in nav
- [ ] Add "Last modified" timestamp
- [ ] Add dark/light mode toggle
- [ ] Add keyboard shortcut help (?)

---

## Completed Tasks

### 2025-10-29
- [x] Fixed backend PM2 config path
- [x] Rounds page visual redesign (teal theme)
- [x] CSS Grid card layout
- [x] Modal button improvements
- [x] SVG placeholder images
- [x] Responsive FAB scaling

### 2025-10-28
- [x] Fixed authentication redirect loop
- [x] Backend build errors resolved
- [x] Initial documentation created

---

## Notes

- Always update this list as tasks complete
- Pair P0 tasks with code changes
- Review weekly during development sessions
- Archive completed tasks monthly

---

*See [../technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md](../technical-debt/DEBT_REGISTER.md) for code quality issues.*

1→# Lazy Golfing Feature Roadmap
     2→
     3→**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
     4→**Research Sources:** See [Competitive Landscape](../research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md) for full analysis
     5→
     6→### Quick Reference - Key Competitors
     7→
     8→| Category | Apps/Platforms |
     9→|----------|----------------|
    10→| **Shot Tracking** | [Arccos](https://www.arccosgolf.com), [Shot Scope](https://www.shotscope.com), [Clippd](https://www.clippd.com), [V1 Golf](https://v1sports.com), [Golf Pad](https://golfpadgps.com) |
    11→| **GPS/Rangefinder** | [18Birdies](https://18birdies.com), [Golfshot](https://golfshot.com), [GolfLogix](https://www.golflogix.com), [Bushnell](https://www.bushnellgolf.com), [Garmin Golf](https://www.garmin.com) |
    12→| **Handicap/Scoring** | [GHIN](https://www.ghin.com), [TheGrint](https://thegrint.com), [SwingU](https://swingu.com), [Hole19](https://www.hole19golf.com) |
    13→| **League/Tournament** | [Golf Genius](https://golfgenius.com), [GolfStatus](https://golfstatus.com), [DoJiggy](https://www.dojiggy.com), [BlueGolf](https://www.bluegolf.com), [Golf GameBook](https://www.golfgamebook.com) |
    14→| **Social/Community** | [MGA Tour](https://mgatour.com), [GolfLync](https://www.sportlync.com), [Deemples](https://deemples.com), [Golf Lounge](https://www.golflounge.app) |
    15→| **Betting/Side Games** | [Beezer Golf](https://beezergolf.com), [Skins App](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/skins-app/id6447497626), [Unknown Golf](https://www.unknowngolf.com), [Golf Bettor](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golf-bettor/id1506651354) |
    16→| **Launch Monitors** | [TrackMan](https://www.trackman.com), [Foresight/GCQuad](https://www.foresightsports.com), [Rapsodo](https://rapsodo.com) |
    17→| **Tee Times** | [GolfNow](https://www.golfnow.com) |
    18→| **Sports Management** | [Project Hockey](https://project.hockey), [TeamSnap](https://www.teamsnap.com) |
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Vision
    23→
    24→Create the most comprehensive golf platform that combines the best features of sports management platforms (like Project Hockey and TeamSnap) adapted specifically for golf. The platform should serve individual golfers, playing groups, leagues, and tournaments.
    25→
    26→---
    27→
    28→## Feature Categories
    29→

    52→

    74→- [ ] Playing partners tagging

# Lazy Golfing Feature Roadmap

**Last Updated:** 2025-12-06
**Research Sources:** See [Competitive Landscape](../research/COMPETITIVE_LANDSCAPE.md) for full analysis

### Quick Reference - Key Competitors

| Category | Apps/Platforms |
|----------|----------------|
| **Shot Tracking** | [Arccos](https://www.arccosgolf.com), [Shot Scope](https://www.shotscope.com), [Clippd](https://www.clippd.com), [V1 Golf](https://v1sports.com), [Golf Pad](https://golfpadgps.com) |
| **GPS/Rangefinder** | [18Birdies](https://18birdies.com), [Golfshot](https://golfshot.com), [GolfLogix](https://www.golflogix.com), [Bushnell](https://www.bushnellgolf.com), [Garmin Golf](https://www.garmin.com) |
| **Handicap/Scoring** | [GHIN](https://www.ghin.com), [TheGrint](https://thegrint.com), [SwingU](https://swingu.com), [Hole19](https://www.hole19golf.com) |
| **League/Tournament** | [Golf Genius](https://golfgenius.com), [GolfStatus](https://golfstatus.com), [DoJiggy](https://www.dojiggy.com), [BlueGolf](https://www.bluegolf.com), [Golf GameBook](https://www.golfgamebook.com) |
| **Social/Community** | [MGA Tour](https://mgatour.com), [GolfLync](https://www.sportlync.com), [Deemples](https://deemples.com), [Golf Lounge](https://www.golflounge.app) |
| **Betting/Side Games** | [Beezer Golf](https://beezergolf.com), [Skins App](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/skins-app/id6447497626), [Unknown Golf](https://www.unknowngolf.com), [Golf Bettor](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/golf-bettor/id1506651354) |
| **Launch Monitors** | [TrackMan](https://www.trackman.com), [Foresight/GCQuad](https://www.foresightsports.com), [Rapsodo](https://rapsodo.com) |
| **Tee Times** | [GolfNow](https://www.golfnow.com) |
| **Sports Management** | [Project Hockey](https://project.hockey), [TeamSnap](https://www.teamsnap.com) |

---

## Vision

Create the most comprehensive golf platform that combines the best features of sports management platforms (like Project Hockey and TeamSnap) adapted specifically for golf. The platform should serve individual golfers, playing groups, leagues, and tournaments.

---

## Feature Categories

### 1. Player Profiles & Stats

#### Personal Dashboard
- [ ] Handicap index display with trend graph
- [ ] Recent rounds summary
- [ ] Personal bests (lowest round, longest drive, most birdies)
- [ ] Season statistics overview
- [ ] Achievement badges/milestones
- [ ] Playing history timeline

#### Detailed Statistics
- [ ] Scoring average (overall, by course, by tee)
- [ ] GIR (Greens in Regulation) percentage
- [ ] FIR (Fairways in Regulation) percentage
- [ ] Putts per round / per hole
- [ ] Sand save percentage
- [ ] Up-and-down percentage
- [ ] Birdie/par/bogey/double distribution
- [ ] Scoring by hole par (par 3s, 4s, 5s)
- [ ] Front 9 vs Back 9 comparison
- [ ] Performance by time of day
- [ ] Performance by weather conditions

#### Player Cards
- [ ] Shareable player profile cards
- [ ] QR code for profile sharing
- [ ] Export stats to PDF

---

### 2. Round Tracking

#### Live Scoring
- [ ] Hole-by-hole scoring during round
- [ ] Real-time leaderboard for playing group
- [ ] GPS distance to pin (via integration)
- [ ] Quick-entry mode for fast scoring
- [ ] Voice input for hands-free entry

#### Post-Round Entry
- [ ] Full scorecard entry
- [ ] Bulk import from other apps
- [ ] Photo upload (scorecards, course photos)
- [ ] Notes per hole
- [ ] Playing partners tagging

#### Round Details
- [ ] Weather conditions (auto or manual)
- [ ] Tee selection (with course rating/slope)
- [ ] Equipment used (clubs, balls)
- [ ] Course conditions notes

---

### 3. Course Database

#### Course Information
- [ ] Course details (name, address, contact)
- [ ] Multiple tee options with ratings/slopes
- [ ] Hole-by-hole data (par, yardage, handicap)
- [ ] Course photos and aerial views
- [ ] Amenities (range, putting green, restaurant)
- [ ] Booking links

#### Course Reviews
- [ ] User ratings (condition, pace, value)
- [ ] Written reviews
- [ ] Photo uploads by users
- [ ] Tips for playing (local knowledge)

#### Course Search
- [ ] Search by location
- [ ] Filter by rating, price, difficulty
- [ ] "Courses near me" with map view
- [ ] Favorites/wishlist

---

### 4. League Management

*Inspired by Project Hockey's league management features*

#### League Setup
- [ ] Dedicated league page/section
- [ ] Custom league branding (logo, colors)
- [ ] League rules configuration
- [ ] Handicap allowance settings
- [ ] Division/flight setup
- [ ] Season archives

#### Scheduling
- [ ] Automated schedule generation
- [ ] Fair/balanced matchup algorithm
- [ ] Integration with course tee time booking
- [ ] Schedule sync to Google/iCal
- [ ] Rainout/reschedule management

#### Standings & Results
- [ ] Live standings updates
- [ ] Tiebreaker configurations
- [ ] Points system customization
- [ ] Week-by-week results
- [ ] Season-long statistics
- [ ] Historical season comparison

#### Team Management
- [ ] Team rosters
- [ ] Captain/manager delegation
- [ ] Sub/replacement player system
- [ ] Player availability tracking
- [ ] Team messaging

---

### 5. Tournament Play

#### Tournament Types
- [ ] Stroke play
- [ ] Match play (bracket management)
- [ ] Scramble/best ball
- [ ] Stableford
- [ ] Skins games
- [ ] Nassau
- [ ] Custom formats

#### Tournament Management
- [ ] Registration system
- [ ] Entry fee collection (payment integration)
- [ ] Handicap verification
- [ ] Flight/division assignments
- [ ] Tee time assignments
- [ ] Starting holes (shotgun starts)

#### Live Tournament Features
- [ ] Live leaderboard
- [ ] Hole-by-hole scoring
- [ ] Live scoring from course
- [ ] Spectator view (read-only leaderboard)
- [ ] Push notifications for key moments
- [ ] Social media sharing

#### Results & Awards
- [ ] Automated prize calculation
- [ ] Results export
- [ ] Certificate generation
- [ ] Photo gallery
- [ ] Winners archive

---

### 6. Playing Groups & Partners

#### Finding Partners
- [ ] Player search by location
- [ ] Skill level matching (handicap range)
- [ ] Availability calendar
- [ ] Playing preferences (pace, betting, etc.)
- [ ] "Looking for game" posts

#### Group Management
- [ ] Create private groups
- [ ] Group chat
- [ ] Group calendar
- [ ] Group statistics/leaderboards
- [ ] Group money games tracking

#### 2-somes, 4-somes, Groups
- [ ] Quick pairing suggestions
- [ ] Group round invitations
- [ ] RSVP tracking
- [ ] Automated reminders

---

### 7. Communication & Notifications

#### Messaging
- [ ] Direct messaging between players
- [ ] Group/team chat
- [ ] League announcements
- [ ] Tournament updates

#### Notifications
- [ ] Push notifications (mobile)
- [ ] Email notifications
- [ ] SMS alerts (optional)
- [ ] Notification preferences

#### Announcements
- [ ] League news/updates
- [ ] Course condition reports
- [ ] Weather alerts

---

### 8. Scheduling & Calendar

#### Personal Calendar
- [ ] Upcoming rounds
- [ ] League dates
- [ ] Tournament schedule
- [ ] Practice sessions
- [ ] Sync to Google/iCal/Outlook

#### Tee Time Integration
- [ ] Course booking links
- [ ] Group tee time coordination
- [ ] Availability polling
- [ ] Reminder notifications

---

### 9. Payments & Finance

#### Payment Collection
- [ ] League dues
- [ ] Tournament entry fees
- [ ] Group money games settlement
- [ ] Stripe/PayPal integration

#### Expense Tracking
- [ ] Green fee tracking
- [ ] Equipment purchases
- [ ] Annual golf expense summary

---

### 10. Mobile Experience

#### Mobile App Features
- [ ] Live scoring during rounds
- [ ] GPS distances
- [ ] Push notifications
- [ ] Quick stats lookup
- [ ] Camera integration for scorecards

#### Offline Support
- [ ] Offline round entry
- [ ] Sync when connected

---

### 11. Social Features

#### Activity Feed
- [ ] Recent rounds from connections
- [ ] League/tournament updates
- [ ] Achievements/milestones
- [ ] Comments and reactions

#### Sharing
- [ ] Share rounds to social media
- [ ] Share player cards
- [ ] Share tournament results

---

### 12. Integrations

#### Data Import
- [ ] GHIN handicap sync
- [ ] Import from other golf apps
- [ ] Bulk CSV import

#### External Services
- [ ] Weather API
- [ ] Course database APIs
- [ ] Mapping services (GPS)
- [ ] Calendar sync

---

## Implementation Phases

### Phase 1: Foundation (Current)
- [x] User authentication (Google OAuth)
- [x] Basic player profiles
- [x] Round tracking (basic)
- [x] Course database (basic)
- [ ] Enhanced statistics

### Phase 2: Community
- [ ] Playing partner search
- [ ] Group management
- [ ] Messaging system
- [ ] Social feed

### Phase 3: Competition
- [ ] League management
- [ ] Tournament system
- [ ] Live scoring
- [ ] Leaderboards

### Phase 4: Advanced
- [ ] Mobile app (PWA or native)
- [ ] Payment integration
- [ ] GHIN integration
- [ ] Advanced analytics

---

## Competitive Differentiation

| Feature | Lazy Golfing | GHIN App | 18Birdies | GolfShot |
|---------|-------------|----------|-----------|----------|
| League Management | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Tournament Play | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Playing Partners | Yes (planned) | No | Yes | No |
| Team/Group Features | Yes (planned) | No | Limited | No |
| Free Tier | Yes | Paid | Freemium | Freemium |
| Custom Branding | Yes (planned) | No | No | No |

---

## Priority Matrix

| Priority | Features | Impact | Effort |
|----------|----------|--------|--------|
| P0 | Basic round tracking, handicap | High | Low |
| P1 | Enhanced stats, groups | High | Medium |
| P2 | Leagues, tournaments | High | High |
| P3 | Live scoring, mobile app | Medium | High |
| P4 | Payments, integrations | Medium | Medium |

---

*This roadmap is a living document. Features will be prioritized based on user feedback and development capacity.*

content.text:
# Start Task - Intelligent Workflow Router

Analyzes your task and automatically routes to the optimal execution strategy per CLAUDE.md's Intelligent Prompt Routing system.

## Routing Logic

**If no task description provided:**
- Run `/check-todos` to list outstanding work
- Await user selection

**If task description provided, analyze and route:**

### 1. Planning Tasks → `/create-plan`
**Keywords:** "plan", "design", "architecture", "multi-phase", "roadmap"
**Complexity:** Multi-phase features, complex architecture
**Example:** "Plan the analytics dashboard feature"

→ Invoke: `/create-plan {{task_description}}`

### 2. Decision-Making → `/consider:*` frameworks
**Keywords:** "should we", "which approach", "evaluate", "compare", "decide"
**Examples:**
- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`

→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework

### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"

→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description

### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
**Criteria:**
- 30min - 2hr estimated work
- 3-10 files to modify
- Clear scope but needs fresh context
**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"

→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`

### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"

→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`

### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
**Criteria:**
- 1-2 files
- <30min work
- Clear, straightforward
**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"

→ Execute directly in current context

## Implementation

**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
```javascript
const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();

if (!taskDescription) {
  // No task provided - check todos
  return "/check-todos";
}
```

**Step 2: Analyze Task**

Examine task description for:
- **Keywords** indicating task type
- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)

**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**

Based on analysis:

```javascript
// Planning tasks
if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
}

// Decision-making tasks
else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
}

// Debugging tasks
else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
}

// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
  // Offer to run after creation
}

// Multi-stage features
else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
}

// Simple/direct - execute in current context
else {
  executeDirectly();
}
```

**Step 4: Follow Through**

After routing:
- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
- If framework: present analysis and next steps

## Examples

```bash
# Automatically routes to /create-plan
/start-task Plan the user authentication system

# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?

# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure

# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
/start-task Refactor the calendar view components

# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach

# Direct execution
/start-task Fix typo in README.md

# Check todos (no argument)
/start-task
```

## Notes

- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed

content.text:
Excellent! Here's a comprehensive analysis of the backend codebase structure:

## lzg.backend TypeScript Files Summary

### File Counts

- **Total TypeScript Files:** 41
- **Test Files (.spec.ts):** 7
- **DTO Files (.dto.ts):** 6
- **Production Source Files:** 28

### Current TypeScript Configuration

The project already has **`"strict": true`** enabled in `tsconfig.json` (line 19). This means the codebase is already configured for strict type checking with:
- `noImplicitAny`
- `strictNullChecks`
- `strictFunctionTypes`
- `strictPropertyInitialization`
- `noImplicitThis`
- And other strict checks

### Module Structure

```
src/
├── Root (4 files)
│   ├── main.ts
│   ├── app.module.ts
│   ├── app.controller.ts
│   ├── app.service.ts
│   └── app.controller.spec.ts
│
├── auth/ (13 files)
│   ├── auth.controller.ts
│   ├── auth.controller.spec.ts
│   ├── auth.service.ts
│   ├── auth.service.spec.ts
│   ├── auth.module.ts
│   ├── google.strategy.ts
│   ├── jwt.strategy.ts
│   ├── jwt-auth.guard.ts
│   ├── admin.guard.ts
│   ├── csrf.guard.ts
│   ├── csrf.util.ts
│   ├── current-user.decorator.ts
│   └── CLAUDE.md (documentation)
│
├── config/ (1 file)
│   └── env.validation.ts
│
├── prisma/ (3 files)
│   ├── prisma.module.ts
│   ├── prisma.service.ts
│   └── prisma.service.spec.ts
│
├── profile/ (5 files)
│   ├── profile.controller.ts
│   ├── profile.service.ts
│   ├── profile.service.spec.ts
│   ├── profile.module.ts
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts
│
├── rounds/ (7 files)
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts
│   ├── rounds.service.ts
│   ├── rounds.service.spec.ts
│   ├── rounds.module.ts
│   └── dto/ (3 files)
│       ├── create-round.dto.ts
│       ├── update-round.dto.ts
│       └── import-csv.dto.ts
│
├── courses/ (7 files)
│   ├── courses.controller.ts
│   ├── courses.service.ts
│   ├── courses.service.spec.ts
│   ├── courses.module.ts
│   └── dto/ (2 files)
│       ├── create-course.dto.ts
│       └── update-course.dto.ts
│
└── audit-log/ (2 files)
    ├── audit-log.module.ts
    └── audit-log.service.ts
```

### Type Definition Organization

**DTOs (Data Transfer Objects):**
1. `src/profile/update-profile.dto.ts`
2. `src/rounds/dto/create-round.dto.ts`
3. `src/rounds/dto/update-round.dto.ts`
4. `src/rounds/dto/import-csv.dto.ts`
5. `src/courses/dto/create-course.dto.ts`
6. `src/courses/dto/update-course.dto.ts`

**Guard/Auth Types:**
- `jwt-auth.guard.ts`
- `admin.guard.ts`
- `csrf.guard.ts`
- `current-user.decorator.ts`

**Service/Controller Types:**
- Each module has typed service and controller files

### Architecture Observations

1. **Strict Mode Status:** Already enabled - no configuration change needed
2. **Type Safety:** Uses class-validator DTOs for input validation across all endpoints
3. **Module Organization:** Clean NestJS modular structure with clear separation of concerns
4. **Guard Pattern:** Comprehensive auth guards (JWT, Admin, CSRF)
5. **Database:** Prisma as the single ORM with global module pattern
6. **Testing:** 7 spec files indicate partial test coverage

### Files by Type

| Category | Count | Files |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Controllers | 5 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, app |
| Services | 6 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, prisma, audit-log |
| DTOs/Validation | 6 | update-profile, 3x round-related, 2x course-related |
| Guards/Auth | 5 | jwt-auth, admin, csrf, current-user, google/jwt strategies |
| Modules | 7 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, prisma, audit-log, app |
| Tests | 7 | Controllers: 2, Services: 5 |
| Utilities | 2 | csrf.util, env.validation |

### Strict Mode Impact Assessment

Since strict mode is already enabled, enabling it would not require changes to compiler options. Any type issues that exist would already be caught by the current configuration. The real work would be:

1. **Ensuring all files pass strict compilation** (run `npm run build`)
2. **Type coverage audit** - Checking for any `any` types or implicit types
3. **DTO completeness** - Ensuring all API inputs are validated
4. **Return type annotations** - Verifying all functions have explicit return types

All absolute file paths are available above for reference.

1. All TypeScript files that exist
2. The module structure
3. Any existing type definitions

List all .ts files in src/ and provide a count. This will help me understand the scope of enabling strict mode.

content.text:
Excellent! Here's a comprehensive analysis of the backend codebase structure:

## lzg.backend TypeScript Files Summary

### File Counts

- **Total TypeScript Files:** 41
- **Test Files (.spec.ts):** 7
- **DTO Files (.dto.ts):** 6
- **Production Source Files:** 28

### Current TypeScript Configuration

The project already has **`"strict": true`** enabled in `tsconfig.json` (line 19). This means the codebase is already configured for strict type checking with:
- `noImplicitAny`
- `strictNullChecks`
- `strictFunctionTypes`
- `strictPropertyInitialization`
- `noImplicitThis`
- And other strict checks

### Module Structure

```
src/
├── Root (4 files)
│   ├── main.ts

│

│

│   └── prisma.service.spec.ts
│
├── profile/ (5 files)

│   ├── profile.service.ts
│   ├── profile.service.spec.ts
│   ├── profile.module.ts
│   └── update-profile.dto.ts
│
├── rounds/ (7 files)
│   ├── rounds.controller.ts
│   ├── rounds.service.ts
│   ├── rounds.service.spec.ts
│   ├── rounds.module.ts
│   └── dto/ (3 files)
│       ├── create-round.dto.ts
│       ├── update-round.dto.ts
│       └── import-csv.dto.ts
│
├── courses/ (7 files)
│   ├── courses.controller.ts
│   ├── courses.service.ts
│   ├── courses.service.spec.ts
│   ├── courses.module.ts
│   └── dto/ (2 files)
│       ├── create-course.dto.ts
│       └── update-course.dto.ts
│
└── audit-log/ (2 files)
    ├── audit-log.module.ts
    └── audit-log.service.ts
```

### Type Definition Organization

**DTOs (Data Transfer Objects):**
1. `src/profile/update-profile.dto.ts`
2. `src/rounds/dto/create-round.dto.ts`
3. `src/rounds/dto/update-round.dto.ts`
4. `src/rounds/dto/import-csv.dto.ts`
5. `src/courses/dto/create-course.dto.ts`
6. `src/courses/dto/update-course.dto.ts`

**Guard/Auth Types:**
- `jwt-auth.guard.ts`
- `admin.guard.ts`
- `csrf.guard.ts`
- `current-user.decorator.ts`

**Service/Controller Types:**
- Each module has typed service and controller files

### Architecture Observations

1. **Strict Mode Status:** Already enabled - no configuration change needed
2. **Type Safety:** Uses class-validator DTOs for input validation across all endpoints
3. **Module Organization:** Clean NestJS modular structure with clear separation of concerns
4. **Guard Pattern:** Comprehensive auth guards (JWT, Admin, CSRF)
5. **Database:** Prisma as the single ORM with global module pattern
6. **Testing:** 7 spec files indicate partial test coverage

### Files by Type

| Category | Count | Files |
|----------|-------|-------|
| Controllers | 5 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, app |
| Services | 6 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, prisma, audit-log |
| DTOs/Validation | 6 | update-profile, 3x round-related, 2x course-related |
| Guards/Auth | 5 | jwt-auth, admin, csrf, current-user, google/jwt strategies |
| Modules | 7 | auth, profile, rounds, courses, prisma, audit-log, app |
| Tests | 7 | Controllers: 2, Services: 5 |
| Utilities | 2 | csrf.util, env.validation |

### Strict Mode Impact Assessment

Since strict mode is already enabled, enabling it would not require changes to compiler options. Any type issues that exist would already be caught by the current configuration. The real work would be:

1. **Ensuring all files pass strict compilation** (run `npm run build`)
2. **Type coverage audit** - Checking for any `any` types or implicit types
3. **DTO completeness** - Ensuring all API inputs are validated
4. **Return type annotations** - Verifying all functions have explicit return types

file.content:
// prisma/schema.prisma

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

// --- Auth/User ---
model User {
  id             String    @id @default(uuid())
  email          String    @unique
  firstName      String
  lastName       String

  // OAuth identity
  provider       String     // e.g., 'google'
  providerId     String     // provider's unique id for the user
  @@unique([provider, providerId])

  // Extra profile fields from providers
  avatarUrl      String?
  locale         String?
  emailVerified  Boolean    @default(false)
  hostedDomain   String?    // e.g., 'company.com' when Google returns hd

  // Auditing
  lastLoginAt    DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime   @updatedAt

  // 1:1 relation (Player holds the FK)
  player         Player?

  // Many-to-many relation with roles
  userRoles      UserRole[]
}

// --- Golf Profile ---
model Player {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
  firstName  String
  lastName   String
  ghinNumber String?
  handicap   Float?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())

  // 1:1 back to User (Player owns FK and it's UNIQUE)
  userId String @unique
  user   User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  rounds Round[]
}

// --- Courses ---
model Course {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())

  // Basic Info
  name            String
  city            String?
  state           String?
  country         String?
  zipCode         String?

  // Par Information (from API or manual)
  totalPar        Int?     // Total 18-hole par
  front9Par       Int?     // Front 9 par
  back9Par        Int?     // Back 9 par

  // Rating Data
  courseRating    Float?   // e.g., 72.4
  slopeRating     Int?     // e.g., 135

  // API Integration
  apiCourseId     Int?     @unique  // ID from golfcourseapi.com
  apiData         Json?    // Complete course data from API (scorecard, tees, yardages, etc.)

  // Geolocation
  latitude        Float?
  longitude       Float?

  // Metadata
  lastSynced      DateTime @default(now())
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt

  rounds Round[]

  @@index([name])
  @@index([city, state])
  @@index([latitude, longitude])
}

// --- Rounds ---
model Round {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  date            DateTime @default(now())
  courseName      String? // DEPRECATED: kept temporarily for migration, will be removed
  courseId        String? // NEW: reference to Course model
  strokes         Int
  front9          Int? // front 9 score (optional)
  back9           Int? // back 9 score (optional)
  playingPartners String? // comma-separated list of playing partners (manual text entry)
  teeTime         DateTime? // tee time for auto-linking rounds
  latitude        Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
  longitude       Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  notes           String? // optional notes
  deletedAt       DateTime? // soft delete support

  // ADVANCED GOLF STATS (12 manual entry fields)

  // Performance Metrics
  girPct          Float?   // Greens in Regulation % (0-100)
  firPct          Float?   // Fairways in Regulation % (0-100)
  approachPct     Float?   // Approach shot accuracy % (0-100)

  // Putting Stats
  front9Putts     Int?     // Front 9 putting
  back9Putts      Int?     // Back 9 putting
  totalPutts      Int?     // Total putts (can be auto-calc from F9+B9 or manual)

  // Scoring Breakdown
  holeInOnes      Int?     // Hole-in-one count
  eagles          Int?     // Eagle count
  birdies         Int?     // Birdie count
  pars            Int?     // Par count
  bogeys          Int?     // Bogey count
  doubleBogeys    Int?     // Double bogey count
  triplePlusBogeys Int?    // Triple bogey+ count

  // who played this round
  playerId String
  player   Player @relation(fields: [playerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  // course reference (optional during migration)
  course     Course? @relation(fields: [courseId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  // round linking relationships
  linkedFrom RoundLink[] @relation("SourceRound")
  linkedTo   RoundLink[] @relation("TargetRound")

  @@index([playerId, date])
  @@index([playerId, deletedAt]) // index for soft delete queries
  @@index([courseId]) // index for course lookups
  @@index([courseId, date, teeTime]) // index for finding potential round matches
}

// --- Round Linking (Playing Partners) ---
model RoundLink {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  sourceRoundId String
  targetRoundId String
  linkType      String   @default("playing_partner") // 'playing_partner', 'tournament', 'league', etc.
  status        String   @default("pending") // 'pending', 'confirmed', 'rejected'
  createdBy     String   // User ID who created the link
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  sourceRound Round @relation("SourceRound", fields: [sourceRoundId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  targetRound Round @relation("TargetRound", fields: [targetRoundId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([sourceRoundId, targetRoundId])
  @@index([sourceRoundId])
  @@index([targetRoundId])
  @@index([status])
}

// --- Role-Based Access Control ---
model Role {
  id          String     @id @default(uuid())
  name        String     @unique // 'admin', 'user', etc.
  description String?
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now())

  userRoles   UserRole[]
}

model UserRole {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  roleId    String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  role Role @relation(fields: [roleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, roleId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([roleId])
}

// --- Audit Logging ---
model AuditLog {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String   // who performed the action
  action    String   // e.g., 'LOGIN', 'LOGOUT', 'CREATE_ROUND', 'UPDATE_PROFILE', 'DELETE_ACCOUNT'
  resource  String?  // e.g., 'Round:uuid', 'Profile:uuid', 'Auth'
  metadata  Json?    // additional context (e.g., IP, user agent, changed fields)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([action, createdAt])
}

content.text:
The backend already has a robust stats endpoint at `GET /v1/me/stats` that returns:
- `totalRounds`, `averageScore`, `bestScore`, `worstScore`
- `bestRound`, `worstRound` with details
- `recentRounds` (last 5)
- `coursesPlayed` (top 5 courses)
- `scoreTrend` for charting

Now I need to check the Round model to see what detailed stats fields exist:

content.content:
1→// prisma/schema.prisma
     2→
     3→datasource db {
     4→  provider = "postgresql"
     5→  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
     6→}
     7→
     8→generator client {
     9→  provider = "prisma-client-js"
    10→}
    11→
    12→// --- Auth/User ---
    13→model User {
    14→  id             String    @id @default(uuid())
    15→  email          String    @unique
    16→  firstName      String
    17→  lastName       String
    18→
    19→  // OAuth identity
    20→  provider       String     // e.g., 'google'
    21→  providerId     String     // provider's unique id for the user
    22→  @@unique([provider, providerId])
    23→
    24→  // Extra profile fields from providers
    25→  avatarUrl      String?
    26→  locale         String?
    27→  emailVerified  Boolean    @default(false)
    28→  hostedDomain   String?    // e.g., 'company.com' when Google returns hd
    29→
    30→  // Auditing
    31→  lastLoginAt    DateTime?
    32→  createdAt      DateTime   @default(now())
    33→  updatedAt      DateTime   @updatedAt
    34→
    35→  // 1:1 relation (Player holds the FK)
    36→  player         Player?
    37→
    38→  // Many-to-many relation with roles
    39→  userRoles      UserRole[]
    40→}
    41→
    42→// --- Golf Profile ---
    43→model Player {
    44→  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
    45→  firstName  String
    46→  lastName   String
    47→  ghinNumber String?
    48→  handicap   Float?
    49→  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
    50→
    51→  // 1:1 back to User (Player owns FK and it's UNIQUE)
    52→  userId String @unique
    53→  user   User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    54→
    55→  rounds Round[]
    56→}
    57→
    58→// --- Courses ---
    59→model Course {
    60→  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
    61→
    62→  // Basic Info
    63→  name            String
    64→  city            String?
    65→  state           String?
    66→  country         String?
    67→  zipCode         String?
    68→
    69→  // Par Information (from API or manual)
    70→  totalPar        Int?     // Total 18-hole par
    71→  front9Par       Int?     // Front 9 par
    72→  back9Par        Int?     // Back 9 par
    73→
    74→  // Rating Data
    75→  courseRating    Float?   // e.g., 72.4
    76→  slopeRating     Int?     // e.g., 135
    77→
    78→  // API Integration
    79→  apiCourseId     Int?     @unique  // ID from golfcourseapi.com
    80→  apiData         Json?    // Complete course data from API (scorecard, tees, yardages, etc.)
    81→
    82→  // Geolocation
    83→  latitude        Float?
    84→  longitude       Float?
    85→
    86→  // Metadata
    87→  lastSynced      DateTime @default(now())
    88→  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
    89→  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt
    90→
    91→  rounds Round[]
    92→
    93→  @@index([name])
    94→  @@index([city, state])
    95→  @@index([latitude, longitude])
    96→}
    97→
    98→// --- Rounds ---
    99→model Round {
   100→  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
   101→  date            DateTime @default(now())
   102→  courseName      String? // DEPRECATED: kept temporarily for migration, will be removed
   103→  courseId        String? // NEW: reference to Course model
   104→  strokes         Int
   105→  front9          Int? // front 9 score (optional)
   106→  back9           Int? // back 9 score (optional)
   107→  playingPartners String? // comma-separated list of playing partners (manual text entry)
   108→  teeTime         DateTime? // tee time for auto-linking rounds
   109→  latitude        Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
   110→  longitude       Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
   111→  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
   112→  notes           String? // optional notes
   113→  deletedAt       DateTime? // soft delete support
   114→
   115→  // ADVANCED GOLF STATS (12 manual entry fields)
   116→
   117→  // Performance Metrics
   118→  girPct          Float?   // Greens in Regulation % (0-100)
   119→  firPct          Float?   // Fairways in Regulation % (0-100)
   120→  approachPct     Float?   // Approach shot accuracy % (0-100)
   121→
   122→  // Putting Stats
   123→  front9Putts     Int?     // Front 9 putting
   124→  back9Putts      Int?     // Back 9 putting
   125→  totalPutts      Int?     // Total putts (can be auto-calc from F9+B9 or manual)
   126→

file.content:
// prisma/schema.prisma

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

// --- Auth/User ---
model User {
  id             String    @id @default(uuid())
  email          String    @unique
  firstName      String
  lastName       String

  // OAuth identity
  provider       String     // e.g., 'google'
  providerId     String     // provider's unique id for the user
  @@unique([provider, providerId])

  // Extra profile fields from providers
  avatarUrl      String?
  locale         String?
  emailVerified  Boolean    @default(false)
  hostedDomain   String?    // e.g., 'company.com' when Google returns hd

  // Auditing
  lastLoginAt    DateTime?
  createdAt      DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt      DateTime   @updatedAt

  // 1:1 relation (Player holds the FK)
  player         Player?

  // Many-to-many relation with roles
  userRoles      UserRole[]
}

// --- Golf Profile ---
model Player {
  id         String   @id @default(uuid())
  firstName  String
  lastName   String
  ghinNumber String?
  handicap   Float?
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())

  // 1:1 back to User (Player owns FK and it's UNIQUE)
  userId String @unique
  user   User   @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  rounds Round[]
}

// --- Courses ---
model Course {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())

  // Basic Info
  name            String
  city            String?
  state           String?
  country         String?
  zipCode         String?

  // Par Information (from API or manual)
  totalPar        Int?     // Total 18-hole par
  front9Par       Int?     // Front 9 par
  back9Par        Int?     // Back 9 par

  // Rating Data
  courseRating    Float?   // e.g., 72.4
  slopeRating     Int?     // e.g., 135

  // API Integration
  apiCourseId     Int?     @unique  // ID from golfcourseapi.com
  apiData         Json?    // Complete course data from API (scorecard, tees, yardages, etc.)

  // Geolocation
  latitude        Float?
  longitude       Float?

  // Metadata
  lastSynced      DateTime @default(now())
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt

  rounds Round[]

  @@index([name])
  @@index([city, state])
  @@index([latitude, longitude])
}

// --- Rounds ---
model Round {
  id              String   @id @default(uuid())
  date            DateTime @default(now())
  courseName      String? // DEPRECATED: kept temporarily for migration, will be removed
  courseId        String? // NEW: reference to Course model
  strokes         Int
  front9          Int? // front 9 score (optional)
  back9           Int? // back 9 score (optional)
  playingPartners String? // comma-separated list of playing partners (manual text entry)
  teeTime         DateTime? // tee time for auto-linking rounds
  latitude        Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
  longitude       Float? // GPS coordinates for location-based linking
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  notes           String? // optional notes
  deletedAt       DateTime? // soft delete support

  // ADVANCED GOLF STATS (12 manual entry fields)

  // Performance Metrics
  girPct          Float?   // Greens in Regulation % (0-100)
  firPct          Float?   // Fairways in Regulation % (0-100)
  approachPct     Float?   // Approach shot accuracy % (0-100)

  // Putting Stats
  front9Putts     Int?     // Front 9 putting
  back9Putts      Int?     // Back 9 putting
  totalPutts      Int?     // Total putts (can be auto-calc from F9+B9 or manual)

  // Scoring Breakdown
  holeInOnes      Int?     // Hole-in-one count
  eagles          Int?     // Eagle count
  birdies         Int?     // Birdie count
  pars            Int?     // Par count
  bogeys          Int?     // Bogey count
  doubleBogeys    Int?     // Double bogey count
  triplePlusBogeys Int?    // Triple bogey+ count

  // who played this round
  playerId String
  player   Player @relation(fields: [playerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  // course reference (optional during migration)
  course     Course? @relation(fields: [courseId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

  // round linking relationships
  linkedFrom RoundLink[] @relation("SourceRound")
  linkedTo   RoundLink[] @relation("TargetRound")

  @@index([playerId, date])
  @@index([playerId, deletedAt]) // index for soft delete queries
  @@index([courseId]) // index for course lookups
  @@index([courseId, date, teeTime]) // index for finding potential round matches
}

// --- Round Linking (Playing Partners) ---
model RoundLink {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  sourceRoundId String
  targetRoundId String
  linkType      String   @default("playing_partner") // 'playing_partner', 'tournament', 'league', etc.
  status        String   @default("pending") // 'pending', 'confirmed', 'rejected'
  createdBy     String   // User ID who created the link
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt

  sourceRound Round @relation("SourceRound", fields: [sourceRoundId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  targetRound Round @relation("TargetRound", fields: [targetRoundId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([sourceRoundId, targetRoundId])
  @@index([sourceRoundId])
  @@index([targetRoundId])
  @@index([status])
}

// --- Role-Based Access Control ---
model Role {
  id          String     @id @default(uuid())
  name        String     @unique // 'admin', 'user', etc.
  description String?
  createdAt   DateTime   @default(now())

  userRoles   UserRole[]
}

model UserRole {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  roleId    String
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  role Role @relation(fields: [roleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@unique([userId, roleId])
  @@index([userId])
  @@index([roleId])
}

// --- Audit Logging ---
model AuditLog {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String   // who performed the action
  action    String   // e.g., 'LOGIN', 'LOGOUT', 'CREATE_ROUND', 'UPDATE_PROFILE', 'DELETE_ACCOUNT'
  resource  String?  // e.g., 'Round:uuid', 'Profile:uuid', 'Auth'
  metadata  Json?    // additional context (e.g., IP, user agent, changed fields)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())

  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([action, createdAt])
}

The database already has all the advanced stats fields:
- **Performance**: `girPct`, `firPct`, `approachPct`
- **Putting**: `front9Putts`, `back9Putts`, `totalPutts`
- **Scoring**: `holeInOnes`, `eagles`, `birdies`, `pars`, `bogeys`, `doubleBogeys`, `triplePlusBogeys`

content.text:
All phases are complete. Let me provide a summary of everything that was implemented:

---

## Summary: All Three Phases Complete

### Phase A: Backend Hardening ✅
1. **TypeScript Strict Mode** - Already enabled (`tsconfig.json` line 19: `"strict": true`)
2. **Database Indexes** - Already implemented (comprehensive indexes in `schema.prisma`)
3. **Unit Tests** - Already comprehensive (7 spec files with 900+ lines of tests)

### Phase B: UX Improvements ✅
1. **Loading States** (`lzg.home/src/components/loading.tsx`)
   - `PageLoader` - Full page spinner with message
   - `Spinner` - Inline spinner for buttons
   - `CardSkeleton`, `ListSkeleton`, `TableRowSkeleton` - Skeleton loaders
   - `ProfileSkeleton`, `RoundSkeleton`, `CourseSkeleton`, `StatsSkeleton` - Domain-specific skeletons

2. **Toast Notifications** (`lzg.home/src/lib/toast.ts`)
   - `toast.success/error/info/warning` - Basic notifications
   - `toast.loading` - Loading state with dismiss/update
   - `toast.promise` - Promise-based notifications
   - Pre-built patterns: `toast.saved()`, `toast.deleted()`, `toast.networkError()`, etc.
   - Added `<Toaster>` to root layout

3. **Delete Confirmation Modals** (`lzg.home/src/components/confirm-dialog.tsx`)
   - `ConfirmDialog` - Reusable confirmation with loading state
   - `DeleteConfirmDialog` - Pre-configured for delete actions
   - `UnsavedChangesDialog` - Pre-configured for unsaved changes

### Phase C: Enhanced Statistics ✅
1. **Statistics Dashboard** (`lzg.home/src/app/(main)/stats/page.tsx`)
   - Key stats grid: Total Rounds, Average Score, Best/Worst Score
   - Empty state with CTA when no rounds
   - Loading skeletons

2. **GIR/FIR/Putts Tracking**
   - Enhanced `profile.service.ts` to aggregate advanced stats
   - Performance metrics cards with color-coded thresholds
   - Shows data availability (e.g., "Based on 5 of 10 rounds")

3. **Scoring Trends and Distributions**
   - Scoring distribution bar chart (hole-in-ones → triple bogeys)
   - Score trend line chart (SVG-based, no external charting lib)
   - Recent rounds list and most played courses

### Additional Changes
- Added `Stats` link to navigation
- Added green brand color to Tailwind config
- Created TypeScript types for stats data
- All shadcn/ui components installed: skeleton, sonner, alert-dialog, dialog, toast, toaster
